Corporate Overview

ABOUT
THE REPORT
L&T Finance Limited, together with its
subsidiaries (referred to as ‘LTF’ or ‘your
Company’), presents its Integrated
Annual Report (referred to as ‘the
Report’) for the financial year ended
March 31, 2026.
The Report marks a pivotal milestone of
your Company’s journey, reflecting the
successful culmination of Lakshya 2026
strategic roadmap, a phase during
which your Company strengthened its
position as a digitally agile and AI-led
financial institution. Building on this
progress, your Company has outlined
its long-term strategic direction under
Lakshya 2031, with a continued focus
to become India’s pre-eminent
Risk-first, Technology-first,
Multi-product Retail Financier of choice.
Central to this transformation has
been emphasis on people, building
capabilities and responsible innovation.
Your Company remains committed to
delivering customer-centric financial
solutions supported by technology,
while maintaining highest standards
of governance and transparency with
focus on stakeholder engagement.
The strategy is anchored in financial
inclusion, environmental stewardship
and social responsibility, and disciplined
execution.

Reporting Principle
The financial information in this Report has been prepared in accordance with the
Companies Act, 2013, applicable Indian Accounting Standards, the requirements
of the Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2006, and the Securities and
Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements)
Regulations, 2015, along with applicable guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank of
India (RBI).
The non-financial disclosures have been prepared in line with globally accepted
frameworks to ensure consistency, comparability, and reliability. The Report
follows the Integrated Reporting (IR) Framework developed by the International
Integrated Reporting Council (IIRC), with disclosures structured across six capitals.
Sustainability disclosures have been prepared with reference to the Global
Reporting Initiative (GRI) 2021 standards for the reporting period April 1, 2025 to
March 31, 2026.
The Report is aligned with:
National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC)
Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting (BRSR) requirements
prescribed by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs)
Incorporating the recommendations of the Task Force on Climate-related
Financial Disclosures (TCFD)

The Report provides a holistic view of
your Company’s performance, covering
financial outcomes, operational
progress, sustainability initiatives,
people priorities, value creation for
shareholders, communities, and
future outlook.

2

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

Scope and
Boundaries
The Report covers the activities of your
Company for the period from April
1, 2025 to March 31, 2026, unless
otherwise stated. The scope includes
the Head Office, branch network
(including rural locations), and relevant
operational footprint during the year.
The reporting boundary is aligned
with the requirements of the Business
Responsibility and Sustainability
Reporting framework (BRSR).
All material disclosures are presented
within the Report or through references
to publicly available information.
Numerical data has been rounded off,
wherever required.

Materiality and
Target Audience
Your Company operates as a
systemically important Non-Banking
Financial Company (NBFC) and plays a
significant role in enabling economic
growth and financial inclusion.

Statutory Reports

Financial Statements

Your Company maintains continuous
engagement with key stakeholders,
including employees, investors,
regulators, customers, partners, and
communities, to ensure that disclosures
remain relevant and decision-useful for
stakeholders.

Approach to
Responsible
Reporting
Your Company adopts a precautionary
approach in its lending and risk
management practices, with a focus
on minimising potential economic,
environmental, and social risks while
enabling sustainable value creation.
The preparation of the Report is
guided by the principles of stakeholder
inclusiveness, materiality, and
completeness, ensuring balanced and
transparent disclosure of performance.

Forward-looking
Statements
The Report contains statements relating
to your Company’s future business
outlook and performance. These
statements may include words such
as ‘expects’, ‘anticipates’, ‘intends’, or
similar expressions.

The Report covers material matters
that have a substantive impact on
your Company’s ability to create value
over time. These have been identified
through a structured materiality
assessment process, incorporating inputs Such statements are based on current
from internal and external stakeholders. assumptions and are subject to risks
and uncertainties, including changes
Your Company follows a double
in economic conditions, regulatory
materiality approach, considering both:
developments, and geopolitical factors.
Impact of business activities on the
Actual outcomes may differ materially
economy, environment, and society, from those expressed or implied.
and;
Your Company does not undertake any
Financial implications of
obligation to update forward-looking
sustainability-related risks and
statements, except as required by
opportunities
applicable laws.

Assurance
The standalone and consolidated annual
financial statements have been audited
by Statutory Auditors.
The non-financial information presented
in the Report has been carefully
reviewed through structured assurance
process. Reasonable assurance on
BRSR Core KPIs and limited assurance
on the select indicators in the BRSR
& Integrated Annual Report (IAR),
respectively has been provided by BDO
India Services Private Limited as per the
SEBI guidelines in accordance with the
ISAE 3000.
The assurance statement forms part of
the Report.

Accessibility and
Feedback
The Report includes cross-references
to relevant sections and additional
information available on your
Company’s website to facilitate ease of
navigation.
Stakeholders may share their feedback
or seek further information by writing to:
igrc@ltfs.com or visit: www.ltfinance.
com/investors

Lakshya Leads.

Technology Propels.
Some years mark progress.
Others define it.
FY26 stands as a defining moment in our journey
where intent is translated into institutional strength,
and ambition is evolved into execution excellence.
As we conclude Lakshya 2026, we do so not as an
endpoint, but as proof of a model that
works-resilient, responsible, and ready for scale.
At the core of this transformation lies a
clear philosophy.

Tech-First.
AI-Led
Momentum Becomes
Advantage
Technology has become the core engine
of transformation
AI-led systems, automation, and data
intelligence are enabling faster, sharper,
and more accurate decision-making,
turning momentum into a competitive
advantage

Risk First
Direction Becomes
Leadership
A risk-intelligent culture is embedded
across the organisation anchored in
foresight, discipline, and governance
Risk is not a control point anymore;
it is the starting point of every
decision, ensuring stability and
sustainable growth

Responsible
Finance
Trust Becomes
Currency

Inclusive
Growth
Scale Becomes
Impact

Growth is anchored in responsibility

Inclusion remains central to the
organisation's progress

Every financial solution is designed
to be transparent, ethical, and
to be aligned with long-term
stakeholder value for strengthening
trust while expanding reach

By expanding access to finance and
enabling underserved communities,
growth is not just faster but
broader and more meaningful

Together, these pillars have shaped Lakshya into more than a strategy, they define how we operate, decide, and grow.

As we enter,

LAKSHYA 2031
we carry forward not just progress, but purpose.
The next phase will be defined by:

Deeper
intelligence

Stronger
execution

Greater
inclusion

Sustainable value
creation

Because at LTF, leadership is demonstrated through action, and progress is propelled through technology.

Lakshya Leads. Technology Propels.

A b o u t Yo u r C o m p a n y
WHO WE ARE

ONE OF THE
LEADING UPPER
LAYER NBFCs
IN INDIA
L&T Finance Limited (LTF),
headquartered in Mumbai,
and part of the illustrious,
Larsen & Toubro Group,
is a leading AI-led NBFC
transforming lending through
digital-first, data-driven
ecosystems and intelligent
credit decisioning. Your
Company empowers emerging
India with responsible financial
solutions across rural and
urban markets.

June 2025 has strengthened your
Company’s retail portfolio, adding
a secured high-yield product and
solidifying your Company’s position as
a comprehensive financial partner for
customers across the country.

Classified as an Upper Layer NBFC
by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI),
your Company maintains a strong
Pan-India presence, reaching ~2 lakh
villages and 450+ cities and towns,
and serving 2.8 Cr+ customer base.
Your Company is one of the
leading Risk-first, Tech-first, Multiproduct Retail Financier, with three
fulcrum products – Rural Group Loans
& Micro Finance (JLG),
Two-Wheeler Finance, and Farm
Equipment Finance, while offering
a wide suite of products including
Personal Loans, Home Loans & Loan
Against Property (LAP), SME Loans,
Micro Loan Against Property (MLAP),
and Gold Finance. The acquisition
of a Gold Finance business in

8

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

1994
Publicly Listed on BSE Limited
and NSE Limited

2011

Vision

Values

LTF envisions to be
an admired and
inspirational financial
institution, creating
sustainable value for
all stakeholders.

Ambition

Discipline

LTF aspires to achieve
something more and
doing better than it did
yesterday.

Creating a culture of results
and not reasons, LTF places
responsibility on doing what
is right day after day.

Pride

Integrity

Confidence in LTF's abilities
as a stable and sustainable
organisation that always
contributes to the customer
and every stakeholder.

Integrity beyond honesty,
including focus, consistency.

Our Core Purpose
India’s growth story is powered
by the aspirations of individuals,
entrepreneurs, farmers, and small
businesses across both urban and rural
markets, many of whom continue to
face challenges in accessing timely,
transparent, and responsible financing.
Your Company exists to bridge this
gap by providing inclusive, technologypowered financial solutions that enable
consumption, entrepreneurship, and
economic participation. By delivering
reliable credit through transparent and
responsible lending and expanding
digital access, your Company aims
to empower livelihoods, strengthen
communities, and contribute
meaningfully to India’s sustainable
economic progress.

Our
Sustainability Vision
To be an environmentally and socially
responsible financial institution built on
the foundation of assurance. We are
committed to generating long-term value
for all our stakeholders.

WHAT
Sets Us Apart
AI-native NBFC
delivering

Risk-first, tech first
DNA with robust
underwriting and
governance

LTF operates a Pan-India distribution network, comprising an extensive branch footprint that enables your Company to serve a
large and diverse customer base across both rural and urban India. Anchored in deep local insights and supported by a scalable
operating model, the network strengthens last-mile connectivity while expanding presence in high-growth urban markets.

C h a i r m a n ’s M e s s a g e

MESSAGE FROM
CHAIRMAN’S DESK

Dear Stakeholders,
I am pleased to report that L&T Finance (LTF) has successfully completed its strategic
evolution into a premier, customer-centric retail finance powerhouse, transforming
lending through digital intelligence. This transformation marks a defining chapter in our
journey, as we pivot our strengths toward the heart of the Indian economy.

The Report captures highlights of
your Company’s steady growth
trajectory and its ‘Risk-First, TechFirst’ approach towards business and
operations. This Integrated Annual
Report also marks a significant
breakthrough for your Company as
it has concluded Lakshya 2026 and
looking forward to its next strategic
chapter, Lakshya 2031. Lakshya 2031
is driven by an execution-oriented
culture emphasizing credit discipline,
rapid tech and AI adoption, and
innovation.

Macroeconomic Scenario
The year 2025 was challenging as
geopolitical conflicts, trade tensions,
and persistent policy uncertainty cast
a shadow over the global economy
and the financial system. Yet India’s
tenacity was visible in strong macro
fundamentals, a large domestic
economy, and a responsive policy
framework that has improved the
economy’s ability to absorb external
shocks. The Indian economy and
the financial system, remained
robust and resilient through FY26,
supported by strong growth, benign
inflation, healthy corporate balance
sheets, sizeable buffers, and prudent
policy reforms.
The finance minister, in the Union
Budget FY27 speech, outlined three
kartavyas towards nation building:
to accelerate and sustain economic
growth, by enhancing productivity
and competitiveness; to fulfil

20

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

aspirations of people and build their
capacity, making them strong partners
in India’s path to prosperity; and to
ensure that every family, community,
region, and sector has access to
resources, amenities, and opportunities
for meaningful participation. These
are in line with the objective of Viksit
Bharat to help create a society that is
prosperous, inclusive, and sustainable
wherein all of its citizens have the
opportunity to thrive and meaningfully
contribute to the nation’s progress.
As India continues on this
transformative agenda, access to
essential financial services, particularly
credit, to every Indian, sits at the heart
of this vision. The implementation
of financial value chain with digital
solutions can enhance credit access,
boost consumption, and promote
equitable growth, thereby contributing
significantly to the vision of Viksit
Bharat 2047.
Today, the financial ecosystem in
India is setting the global benchmark
for access, innovation, and customer
engagement. The widespread
penetration of UPI, Aadhaar-enabled
payment system, and a full range
of digital solutions have established
a robust interoperative financial
network. The sector is also witnessing a
paradigm shift as Artificial Intelligence
and GenAI have become pivotal
in driving innovation. I am pleased
to note that your Company is at
the forefront of leveraging cuttingedge technologies, including AI

applications and data analytics, in
enhancing decision-making processes
and optimizing customer experience
and pioneering a high-tech, hightouch model of inclusive finance and
development across society.

Year Under Review
Delivering on Lakshya 2026: A
Milestone Year
This past year tested the financial
sector with significant macroeconomic
events especially for the microfinance
segment and the geopolitical
headwinds, yet your Company
demonstrated exceptional resilience.
This was achieved through unwavering
commitment, disciplined execution,
and by implementing advanced
proprietary AI tech stacks built inhouse. This includes AI engines like
Project Cyclops for credit underwriting
and Project Nostradamus, for risk
assessment, early warning signals, and
portfolio monitoring, thereby helping
your Company to reduce credit costs
and improve portfolio quality.

Expansion into Gold Finance:
A Strategic Opportunity

Performance against Lakshya
2026 Targets
Retailisation
Your Company successfully
transformed to a retail franchise by reorienting itself to achieve retailisation
of 98% in Q4FY26 against the target
of >95% (FY22 – 51%) with the retail
book crossing the ₹1 lakh Cr milestone.
Retail Book Growth
Against the goal of achieving a retail
asset book growth of >25% CAGR
over the four-year plan period, your
Company has achieved a CAGR of
28% thus exceeding its Lakshya 2026
goals.

With the objective of diversifying
its product offerings and balancing
the secured, unsecured asset ratio
favorably, your Company forayed
into a secured high-margin segment,
i.e., Gold Finance, by completing the
successful acquisition of the Gold
Finance business of Paul Merchants.
Within 8 months of acquisition, the
Company has added over 200 new
branches, doubling its Gold Finance
branch network to over 330 branches
nationwide.
As industry-wide gold loan
outstandings crossed the historic
₹4 lakh Cr mark as of January 31,
2026, growing at 128% year-on-year,
your Company is strategically poised to
capitalise on this opportunity, especially
in light of the rapid expansion of your
Company’s gold loan branch network.
This growth is a dual victory: it provides
your Company’s balance sheet with
high-quality, secured assets while
offering our customers a vital 'tailwind'
for their personal and business needs.

Asset Quality
Despite a challenging macroeconomic
environment, your Company
maintained the consolidated asset
quality within the set goals of Gross
Stage 3 (GS3) <3% and Net Stage 3
(NS3) < 1% by FY26. Your Company
achieved a GS3 of 2.88% and NS3 of
0.96% in Q4FY26.
Return on Assets (RoA)
Against the target of achieving a
consolidated RoA of 2.8% to 3.0%
by FY26, despite the prevailing

22

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

Gold Finance business does more than
just diversify your Company’s assets; it
creates a powerful synergy for cross-sell
with existing customer base in Rural
Group Loan and Microfinance, Farmer
Finance and Two-Wheeler Finance who
have substantial outstanding Gold
Finance.
By leveraging the significant overlap
within our existing customer base,
we are uniquely positioned to
deepen our relationships and provide
a comprehensive suite of financial
solutions to those we already serve.
This transition from a single-product
relationship to a holistic financial
partnership is not just a growth
milestone but a foundational pillar for
our next phase of value creation.
Further, your Company with an aim of
creating a comprehensive distribution
model and driving operating leverage,
is taking steps to enhance its offerings
at the gold loan branches. With the
inauguration of first Sampoorna
branch, your Company has begun a
strategic evolution from single-product
touchpoints to integrated service hubs.
This model shifts your Company’s focus
from transactional lending to holistic
relationship management, enhancing
customer lifetime value by positioning
every branch as a gateway to our
full suite of financial solutions. To
date, your Company has successfully

established 30 Sampoorna branches
across cities.

Maximizing Profitability
Through Advanced Analytics
and Operational Efficiency
Your Company continues to drive
sustainable profitability through
a strong thrust on productivity
enhancement and cost optimization,
underpinned by advanced data and
digital analytics. We have broadened
our customer acquisition funnel and
enhanced cross-selling capabilities by
deploying futuristic digital architecture.
Innovations like the 'Loan Offer Pod'
strengthen the cross-sell engine, while
proprietary AI models like Project
Cyclops and Project Nostradamus
optimize credit underwriting and risk
management. This tech-first approach
eliminates chokepoints and boosts
operational efficiencies, ensuring
growth remains structurally sound and
resilient.
The implementation of Project Cyclops
and Project Nostradamus has delivered
a profound business impact, through
superior credit performance and highly
resilient credit indicators. In the TwoWheeler segment, the Project Cyclops
portfolio significantly outperformed
industry peers by maintaining a
delinquency rate of just 7%. Such
precision highlights a stark departure
from broader market trends, where
delinquency rates hit 29% for Banks,
41% for NBFCs, and 20% for Captive
Financiers.
Beyond credit quality, these AI
interventions have driven substantial
gains in operational efficiency by
streamlining the underwriting process.
Within SME Finance, the combined
power of Project Cyclops and the
Project Helios co-pilot drastically
reduced underwriting Turnaround
Time (TAT). Specifically, TAT for SelfEmployed Professionals dropped
from 21 hours to 14 hours, while the
processing time for Self-Employed

Statutory Reports

Non-Professionals was cut from
37 hours to 25 hours, enabling a
more agile and responsive lending
environment.

PLANET App - Journeys and
Achievements
PLANET app has been highly
transformative, emerging as a powerful
digital channel with over 2.4 Cr
downloads facilitating end-to-end
digital loan journeys and providing
free access to credit scores. Validating
our focus on global-standard user
experience, it won the "Best Digital
Experience in Finance" award at the
Global Fintech Fest 2025.
PLANET 3.0 introduced features like a
multi-lingual conversational voice agent
and Do-it-Yourself (DIY) journeys for
settlements. With over 236 servicing
options, it acts as a force multiplier,
accelerating cross-sell volumes by 4x
and new-to-LTF volumes by 3x.
The new addition this year was launch
of Partner PLANET to offer a complete
service suite for our dealer partners.
This enables seamless, end-to-end
digital journeys, real-time servicing, and
enhanced transparency, significantly
accelerating digital adoption across
segments.

Amplifying Brand Presence

Geographical Expansion

This year, your Company significantly
amplified its brand presence through
high-impact integrated marketing
campaigns with brand ambassador
Jasprit Bumrah for two businesses,
i.e., Two-Wheeler Finance, achieving
a reach of 80% (76 mn) in campaign
markets, resulting in a total
spontaneous awareness increase to
38% from 15%. Similarly, Business
Loans achieved a reach of 80% (8.8
mn) in campaign markets, resulting in
a total spontaneous awareness increase
to 31% from 12%. By leveraging the
influence of brand ambassador, your
Company achieved heightened visibility
and deepened customer engagement.
The brand association scores for
Retail, Digital, and Sustainable were
32% in FY26. The LTF brand's reach
through digital was 168 mn at 16%
engagement rate.

Your Company significantly scaled its
geographic footprint across multiple
business verticals, marked by a strategic
push into diverse new territories.
The Gold Finance division led this
expansion by entering eight new
regions, including Assam, Karnataka,
Telangana, Maharashtra, Odisha,
West Bengal, Himachal Pradesh, and
Rajasthan increasing its total reach
to 18 States and Union Territories.
Similarly, the Two-Wheeler Finance
segment strengthened its presence
in Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Goa,
and Western Uttar Pradesh, while the
Home Loan and Loan Against Property
division extended its services into
Assam and Bihar. Growth also reached
more specialized markets, with SME
Finance launching operations in Jammu
and Meghalaya, and Rural Business
Finance marking its strategic re-entry
into Assam.

Your Company’s flagship BFSI-AI event,
‘RAISE,’ saw record-breaking success
with over 4,500 attendees and over
40 expert speakers. It helped reach
a 16 million audience with 1 mn
engagements. Furthermore, the launch
of ‘Pitch Point,’ a pioneering AI startup
platform, reinforced your Company’s
commitment to nurturing the fintech
AI ecosystem. Your Company amped
up brand visibility through standardized
branding at over 140 Gold Finance
branches.

Sustainability and Social Impact
Sustainability and social responsibility
remain central to your Company’s
long-term value creation and strategic
direction. Since embarking on its
sustainability journey in 2018–19, your
Company has steadily strengthened its
commitment to responsible growth.
In FY26, this commitment was
recognized with your Company being
rated as the Most Sustainable NBFC
by BusinessWorld, while continuing
to maintain best-in-class ESG ratings.
As a signatory to the United Nations
Global Compact, your Company aligns
its strategies with globally accepted
principles of responsible business
conduct.
Your Company remains firmly on
track to achieve carbon neutrality
by 2035. Focused environmental
initiatives including rooftop solar
pilots across branches, energy audits,
enhanced efficiency measures, and
green infrastructure adoption reflect
a disciplined approach to climate
action. Capabilities to measure and
manage financed emissions have been
strengthened through association with
the Partnership for Carbon Accounting
Financials, while maintaining a
water-surplus status underscores your
Company’s commitment to resource
stewardship.

your Company extended relief support
to over 1 lakh individuals affected by
floods.
These efforts reflect your Company’s
unwavering commitment to building
resilient communities and advancing
sustainable development at scale,
while creating enduring value for all
stakeholders.

MESSAGE FROM
MANAGING DIRECTOR AND
CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER’S DESK

Dear Stakeholders,
I would like to extend my deepest gratitude to the Board members for their continued
support and guidance throughout the year. The journey of L&T Finance (LTF) through
FY26 has been truly transformative, driven by the exceptional talent of our teams who
fuel our ambitious vision of becoming a Risk-first, Technology-first Multi-product Retail
Financier of choice in the country.
Speaking about FY26, the year
presented a complex economic
landscape characterised by elevated
global uncertainties, geopolitical
tensions and sector-specific headwinds,
particularly in the microfinance
segment. However, despite the
headwinds, we remained steadfast
in our approach of tightening credit
and risk administration frameworks,
strengthening collections infrastructure,
accelerating our AI-led technology
transformation and continuously
focusing on growth across all our
business lines. This was positively
supported by favourable domestic
macroeconomic conditions - robust
rural demand, favourable monsoons,
and the catalyst of GST 2.0 reforms—
which provided businesses a strong
foundation to navigate these
challenges. For LTF, this past year was
not just about weathering uncertain
and volatile business environment;
it was about seizing opportunities,
achieving groundbreaking milestones
like highest ever annual retail
disbursements and crossing the ₹1.20
lakh Cr consolidated book, while
transforming the entity to a prime retail
franchise.
We witnessed numerous ‘firsts’
throughout the year that actively
future-proofed our organisation. We
scaled Project Cyclops, our proprietary
AI and Machine Learning-based
credit underwriting engine, across
multiple businesses at record speed,
and launched Project Nostradamus,

26

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

our industry-first AI-driven portfolio
management engine. We further
empowered our workforce with nextgeneration tools like Project Helios,
and Project Orion. Additionally, we
deepened strategic alliances with
Big Tech platforms, and successfully
integrated and rapidly expanded our
newly acquired Gold Loan business
while also launching and scaling our
multi-product 'Sampoorna' branches.
During the year, we maintained a
sharp focus on capability-building,
proudly earning our Great Place To
Work® recertification with an improved
employee satisfaction score of 84
and an impressive 89% participation
rate. We amplified our brand visibility
through impactful campaigns featuring
Jasprit Bumrah and successfully hosted
the second edition of RAISE'25, our
flagship BFSI-AI summit, which has
successfully established our position as
a premier AI thought leader in the BFSI
space in the country. We also solidified
our commitment to sustainability,

with over 41% of our loan book now
dedicated to financing sustainable
livelihoods.
Despite the broader slowdown in certain
unsecured and microfinance lending
segments, your Company maintained
a calibrated, sustainable growth
momentum. This resilience continues
to be driven by our robust five-pillar
framework of execution. This Integrated
Annual Report details how we are
actively charting our course toward
our Lakshya 2026 goals, while laying
the groundwork for our upcoming
Lakshya 2031 vision. The ‘Management
Discussion and Analysis’ section provides
a detailed account of our significant and
measurable progress across these critical
areas.

Achievements under
Lakshya 2026
FY26 also marks the successful
completion of our Lakshya 26 strategic
plan, achieving most of our stated
objectives even amid volatility in the
credit environment. This reflects the
resilience of our diversified franchise,
disciplined execution, and the strength
of the digital and analytics capabilities
that we built during the plan period.
Under the Lakshya 2026 strategy, our
primary objective of achieving >95%
Retailisation has been surpassed,
reaching 98%. As of FY26, our
retail book stands at ₹1,19,508 Cr,
representing a robust 26% Year-on-Year

L&T Finance

27

Corporate Overview

(YoY) growth. We have outpaced our
retail growth target of 25%.
On the asset quality front, we remain
disciplined. Our consolidated NS3
stands at 0.96%, well within our
<1% threshold, GS3 remains stable at
2.88%. While our Return on Assets
(RoA) reached 2.39% (before the
impact of Labour Code) for FY26, we
are firmly committed to achieving a
consolidated target of 2.8%-3% in
the medium term.

Granular Execution on
the 5-Pillar Strategy in
FY26
The first pillar of our strategic
roadmap is Enhancing Customer
Acquisition. I am happy to share
that throughout the year, our focus
remained on maintaining customer
acquisition momentum both vertically
and horizontally while proactively
implementing credit adjustments to
ensure sustained portfolio quality,
which enabled us to successfully
broaden our customer funnel and
increased velocity across both rural
and urban geographies.
By strategically expanding our
distribution footprint, our Company
capitalised on the GST 2.0
implementation and a favourable
monsoon to capture robust festive
demand, driving our new customer
acquisition to the highest ever
customer addition of 8.3 lakh
customers in the last quarter of
FY26, taking our overall customer
franchise stands at over 2.8 Cr unique
customers, one of the highest in the
BFSI space. A major highlight of this
pillar is our Gold Finance business
expansion. Since its acquisition, we
rapidly gained momentum in Gold
Finance branches expansion, exiting
Q4FY26 with 330 branches, addition
of 200 new branches since acquisition
of the business in June 2025. Out
of the 330 gold loan branches, 30
locations have been integrated into

28

the multi-product Sampoorna branch
network. In FY27, we intend to deploy
over 400 new gold loan branches, and
at least 100 of these are targeted to be
Sampoorna branches.
Furthermore, we are aggressively
leveraging our massive database of over
2.8 Cr customers. Through targeted
cross-sell and up-sell initiatives, we
continue to efficiently harvest this base,
building a dominant, highly scalable
retail franchise.
As of FY26, the Company has
established a vast expansion of its
presence across various geographic
levels in India. The company operates its
urban network across 23 states, while
its rural network is present in 18 states.
Additionally, the Company's urban
network covers 4 Union Territories, and
it has established its presence in more
than 450 cities and towns across the
country. Through its extensive rural
franchise, the company reaches around
2 lakh villages. To support this panIndia presence, the company leverages
a granular distribution network
comprising over 14,000 distribution
touchpoints, which includes a total of
673 branches (144 Rural and 529 Urban
branches).
The second pillar of our strategy is
Sharpening Credit Underwriting.
We have revolutionised our risk
assessment through Project Cyclops,
our proprietary AI-based credit engine.
Now operationalised across TwoWheeler Finance, Farm Equipment
Finance, SME Finance, and Personal
Loans, Project Cyclops leverages over
55 algorithms to drive highly accurate
underwriting at over 1,400 transactions
per second. This empowers us to build
a prime-dominant, resilient portfolio
while significantly reducing credit costs.
Complementing this, we launched
Project Nostradamus for proactive
portfolio management and introduced
Project Helios, our AI co-pilot that
cuts SME assessment times to under

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

30 minutes. These cutting-edge tech
interventions maintain our industryleading asset quality.
Our third pillar, Implementing Futuristic
Digital Architecture, drives our vision of
creating a top-class Fintech@Scale. Our
commitment to digital transformation
remains centered on the customer, with a
primary focus on optimising every digital
journey to eliminate chokepoints and
deliver a truly superlative experience. By
moving toward an in-house engineering
model, we have significantly enhanced
our agility and reduced time-to-market
for new features. This strategic shift
not only allows us to maintain greater
control over our technological ecosystem
but also ensures that we are positioned
to respond rapidly to evolving market
demands with precision and excellence.
With over 2.3 Cr downloads, the PLANET
app now offers real-time, multi-lingual
voice servicing and won “Best Digital
Experience in Finance” at the 2025
Global Fintech Fest. Recent expansions
include the Partner PLANET app for
dealers and the Loan Offer Pod digital
cross-sell engine. Operational efficiency
is further enhanced by Knowledgeable
AI (KAI), an agentic solution managing
automated collection calls in 11
languages across multiple business lines.
These initiatives solidify LTF’s commitment
to innovation and superior customer
journeys within the Banking, Financial
Services, and Insurance (BFSI) sector.
Heightened Brand Visibility, the fourth
pillar of our strategy, was prioritised
across all channels to support retail
expansion and our shift toward the
customer. Focused on expanding
awareness and market presence, the
company leveraged brand ambassador
Jasprit Bumrah through targeted, techdriven campaigns. The ‘Aapke Business
Ka Game Changer’ integrated marketing
campaign for Business Loans utilised
AI-generated posters to turn MSME
owners into micro-PR agents, driving
organic advocacy. This was supported
by a strategic media mix, including TVCs

on business news channels, hoardings
in major SME clusters, and premium
branding at airports and in-flight
magazines.
The digital microsite, www.
ltfgamechanger.in, further boosted
engagement via AI-powered tools.
Following the acquisition of the Gold
Finance business, LTF rebranded existing
locations and doubled its network to
over 260 branches within eight months.
During the year, we ran integrated
marketing campaigns for our TwoWheeler Finance, the "Just Zoom"
campaign equated Bumrah’s bowling
speed with rapid, AI-driven approvals.
This was timed with the Asia Cup
'25, where LTF served as an associate
sponsor, blending cricket and festive
seasons to position credit as an
aspirational lifestyle enabler. These
integrated efforts successfully combined
high-impact visibility with niche
audience engagement across all core
product lines.
The fifth pillar of our strategic roadmap
is Capability Building and Employee
Initiatives. On the capability building
front, we have taken a series of
measures during the year. We have
completed our annual long-range
planning cycle across all verticals
reinforcing the alignment of functionlevel talent strategies with our
overarching business objectives. On the
employee initiative front, we launched
the employee engagement service,
including the third edition of the Great
Place to Work survey, to enable the
management to effectively listen to the
voice of its employees.
To combat evolving digital fraud like
phishing and deepfakes, LTF has
launched ‘Sachet with Sachet,’ a cyber
safety initiative led by the animated
mascot Sachet Kumar. This campaign
simplifies complex security concepts
through a dedicated microsite,
LTFSachet.in, which serves as a central
hub for financial awareness.

Industry Titans and Tech

RAISE’25
I am also delighted to announce that

edition of our flagship BFSI-AI event
RAISE’25 in November, with the theme
‘Accelerating Financial Services with
AI,’ which attracted 4,500 attendees
and 40 speakers. The event united
industry leaders, including Mr. S.N.

fintech AI entrepreneur ecosystem.

Performance of Retail
Finance Businesses
Today, your Company stands as one of
the market leaders, a top financier in
its fulcrum businesses, built carefully
and strategically over the years, while
rapidly making its mark in new business
segments. Let’s look at the performance
of key businesses in FY26.

Rural Business Finance
The RBF business, which went
through an adverse cycle in H1FY26
witnessed disbursements returning
to pre-crisis levels, registering annual
disbursements at ₹25,882 Cr, up
24% YoY. Performance was boosted
by continuous efforts on improving
collection efficiencies, which reached
99.8% by the end of FY26 and
tapping disbursement opportunities as
regional event risks abated. We remain
committed to disciplined expansion
through internal guardrails to manage
legislative variances across states.

Farmer Finance
In this business segment, annual
disbursements for FY26 stood at ₹8,674
Cr, up 9% YoY. Growth was catalyzed
by favorable monsoon conditions and
the strategic impact of GST 2.0 reforms,
which lowered tractor tax rates and
significantly boosted demand.

Urban Finance
This segment experienced a strong
performance shift. In FY26, annual
disbursements for Two-Wheeler Finance
stood at ₹10,787 Cr, up 16% YoY and
in Personal Loans stood at ₹12,220 Cr,
up 100% YoY. We successfully scaled
high-growth areas by leveraging festive
demand, regulatory changes, and
strengthened digital partnerships.

Corporate Governance

Governance Philosophy

GOVERNANCE
THAT BUILDS TRUST
LTF’s governance framework, rooted in the enduring ethos of the L&T Group,

Governance at LTF is designed to evolve in line with changes in the business environment, adapting to shifts in the
regulatory landscape and stakeholder expectations. This dynamic approach ensures that governance frameworks remain
relevant and effective.

provides the stability necessary to pursue long-term growth with unwavering
discipline. Your Company’s governance framework enables robust oversight,
transparent disclosures, and effective risk management practices. These
measures ensure responsible operations while consistently safeguarding
stakeholder interests.

Within this framework, the Board provides strategic guidance to strengthen governance practices and processes across the
organisation, while ensuring that ethical conduct is embedded across strategic and operational decisions. It also actively
identifies and addresses emerging risks in a timely manner, with regular reviews of policies, processes, and controls further
strengthening institutional resilience. Your Company actively engages with investors, employees, customers, suppliers, and
communities, recognising that their trust and support is critical for its success.
Your Company integrates the core values of ethics, transparency, accountability and responsible business conduct into its
operations. Thereby ensuring strong governance practices, regulatory compliance and transparent disclosures.
Your Company's governance philosophy serves as the foundation of its decision making, reinforcing its commitment to
ethical leadership, transparency, and long-term sustainable value creation.

Corporate Structure

As your Company accelerates its growth journey, it remains anchored in a risk-first, ethics-led approach that strengthens
resilience, enhances transparency, and reinforces long-term stakeholder trust. By safeguarding stakeholder value while
scaling with discipline, your Company successfully achieved its Lakshya 2026 goals. This milestone lays a strong foundation
for a sustained momentum toward LTF’s Lakshya 2031 vision.
The governance framework ensures regulatory compliance while embedding integrity and responsible operations across all
business functions. It also strengthens oversight mechanisms and integrates strategic foresight into operations. Together,
these elements support sustainable growth while maintaining accountability and organisational discipline.

Governance Structure

Board Committees

Your Company’s governance structure follows a multi-layered framework designed to promote transparency, strengthen
oversight and ensure accountability across all levels of organisation. It processes key decisions through Board Committees
with clearly defined roles and responsibilities. Internal working sub-committees and groups further support these
Committees with cross-functional expertise.

In accordance with the Companies Act, 2013, SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations,
2015 (‘SEBI Listing Regulations’), and applicable RBI regulations, the Board of Directors (‘Board’) has constituted the
following Committees.

This framework integrates senior management inputs with formal Board oversight to strengthen decision-making. This
approach creates a continuous cycle of informed insight and disciplined decision-making across all levels of the organisation.
As a result, your Company ensures consistency, accountability, and effective governance throughout its operations.

mechanisms in

Driving Excellence
through
Governance
Governance at LTF operates through a
structured decision-making framework
that ensures proper evaluation at
each level. This framework enables
timely assessment of matters fostering
informed decision making and prudent
risk management.
Initially, defined internal and grouplevel mechanisms assess key matters
through detailed analysis and crossfunctional inputs. Subsequently,
these matters are placed before
the appropriate Board Committees
for focused review and strategic
oversight. Following Committee
review, recommendations are placed
before Board, as appropriate. The
guidance from the Board/Committees
are implemented through further
strengthening of processes and
controls.

Thus, the framework is anchored
by clearly defined policies, authority
matrices, and Committee charters
or terms of reference. These ensure
consistency, discipline, adaptability and
robust governance across all processes.

Board Oversight
and Governance
Leadership
At the core of LTF’s governance
framework, the Board serves as the
highest governing authority. It provides
strategic direction, management
oversight, and supervision across all
operations. The Board also ensures
accountability while safeguarding the
interests of all stakeholders.
Operating within a dynamic and
highly regulated financial services
environment, the Board plays a
critical role. Its responsibilities extend
beyond statutory oversight to include

long-term value creation and ethical
conduct. The Board also focuses on
organisational resilience to ensure
sustained performance.
Through informed supervision and
collective judgement, the Board
strengthens the governance practices
across the organisation. It ensures
alignment with regulatory expectations,
business priorities, and evolving
stakeholder needs. This reinforces a
strong governance framework that
supports sustainable growth and
responsible decision-making.

Board Composition
and Independence
An effective Board structure remains
central to strong governance, enabling
balanced judgement, independent
assessment and informed decisionmaking. The Board comprises members
with diverse experience and expertise.
This diversity strengthens oversight

In this context, the Board Diversity
Policy promotes a well-rounded
and balanced Board composition.
It encourages an appropriate mix
of skills, experience, gender, age
and professional backgrounds. This
approach enhances the quality of
deliberations and improves overall
governance discipline. As a result,
the Board remain better equipped to
address complex business challenges.
As on March 31, 2026, the Board of
LTF comprised seven Directors, with an
appropriate mix of executive, nonexecutive, and independent members,
in compliance with the Companies
Act, 2013, SEBI Listing Regulations,
and applicable RBI guidelines. The
Board brings together members
with diverse skills and competencies,
enriched by experience across the
public and private sectors, including
professionals and academicians,
further strengthened by expertise
in sustainability and information
technology.

The Board continues to review and
strengthen its governance practices
and could consider additional
appointments, where appropriate,
to further enhance independence
and oversight, ensuring alignment
with evolving regulatory and business
expectations.
A majority-independent Board
supported by majority of Board
Committees being chaired by
Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

Financial Statements

of your Company’s governance and
strategic priorities.

Independent Directors constitute the
majority of the Board, strengthening
its objectivity and enhancing oversight
of management and governance
processes, while supporting disciplined
decision-making across strategic and
risk-related matters.

Independent Directors reinforces governance credibility and promotes constructive
challenge. It ensures that strategic and risk-related decisions are evaluated
with strong accountability. The structure also balances executive insights with
independent oversight. As a result, stakeholder interest remains central to all key
decisions.

Role of the Board
The Board provides strategic leadership and ensures strong governance oversight across the organisation. It guides your
Company’s long-term direction while aligning decisions with overall business objectives.
At the same time, the Board balances growth ambitions with prudent risk management practices. It also ensures regulatory
compliance and accountability across all levels.
Key responsibilities of the Board include:

Strategic Direction
Setting your Company’s long-term vision, approving strategic priorities, and reviewing progress against
defined objectives

Risk and Capital Oversight
Overseeing the risk appetite framework, capital discipline, and key financial and non-financial risks to ensure
sustainable growth

Appointment/Reappointment of Directors

Board Effectiveness and Performance

All appointments and reappointments to the Board are conducted in compliance with applicable legal provisions.
A structured and transparent process is designed to ensure continuity, independence, and the availability of appropriate skills
at the Board level.

In a constantly shifting business environment, the Board serves as a steady anchor. It guides direction, balancing priorities and
responds to emerging complexities. At the same time, it remains focused on your Company’s core purpose and long-term
aspirations. Beyond oversight, the Board actively shapes strategic choices and ensures that your Company remains adaptable,
resilient, and forward-looking.

As per regulatory requirements, the reappointment of Directors who retire by rotation is subject to the approval of
shareholders. Each Board member is elected through individual voting. The process for resignation or removal of Directors,
including Independent Directors, is governed by sections 168 and 169 of the Companies Act, 2013, as well as the applicable
SEBI Listing Regulations. None of the Independent Directors are due for re-appointment in FY27.

The effectiveness of this stewardship is anchored in three interdependent levers. Collectively, these elements enable the
Board to operate with agility while maintaining strategic focus.

People

Architecture

Processes

Driving Clarity and Insight

Embedding Discipline and

The Board’s strength stems from the
collective capability of its members.
It brings together individuals with
complementary skills, diverse industry
experience, and diverse perspectives.
This combination enables richer
deliberations and more balanced
outcomes.
At the same time, LTF reinforces this
diversity, with clear expectations around
preparedness and accountability.
Directors are continuously engaged
with evolving business priorities,
regulatory developments, and industry
trends. As a result, their contributions
stay informed and forward-looking.
Such engagement allows the Board to
challenge ideas constructively while
providing effective guidance. It also
ensures that decisions reflect both
rigour and foresight.

Effective governance depends on the
quality of information that reaches the
Board. The focus remains on relevance,
clarity, and timeliness rather than
volume of information shared. This
approach ensures that key issues are
surfaced, contextualised, and prioritised
for a meaningful discussion.
Information flows are designed to
distil complexity into actionable
insight for decision-making. This is
further strengthened by integrating
external viewpoints, whether through
market signals, peer benchmarking
or broader stakeholder perspectives.
Together, they provide the Board with
a comprehensive understanding of the
operating environment. Continuous
updates beyond formal meetings keep
members connected with organisational
developments and priorities. As a result,
the Board stays engaged, proactive,
and better equipped to guide the
organisation effectively.

Robust governance is anchored in
well-defined structures and consistent
processes, enabling independent
oversight and accountability. It
ensures that decision-making remains
transparent, objective, and aligned
with organisational priorities. Critical
functions such as Risk, Compliance,
and Internal Audit operate with direct
reporting to Board-level committees.
This reporting structure reinforces
accountability across all governance
layers.
This structural clarity is supported by
a disciplined cadence of evaluations
and strategic reviews. Regular risk
assessments ensure that governance
remains continuous and forwardlooking. These mechanisms collectively
strengthen the organisation’s ability to
anticipate and manage emerging risks
effectively.

Regular engagement with Senior Management, along with on-ground exposure through field interactions, bridges the gap
between strategy and execution, allowing the Board to stay closely aligned with operational realities while guiding long-term
direction.
These mechanisms are further reinforced through a structured performance evaluation framework, enabling continuous
improvement and accountability at the Board and Committee levels.

Board Capability and Expertise
The Board brings together a broad mix of skills aligned with your Company’s operations. It remains committed to upholding high
standards of governance across all aspects of its operations. LTF consistently strives to create sustainable long-term value for all
stakeholders. It ensures comprehensive compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations, as well as adoption of best practices.
The governance culture is anchored in accountability, transparency and ethical conduct. These principles guide all strategic and
operational decisions across the organisation. They also strengthen confidence among stakeholders and regulatory bodies. The Board
understands your Company’s operating context and strategic priorities. This understanding enables informed and balanced oversight.
These capabilities are applied in an integrated manner, allowing the Board to assess interlinkages across growth, risk, technology, and
sustainability.

Empowering the
Board: Knowledge,
Insight, Impact
Recognising the importance of
continuous capability building, your
Company has established a structured
learning and development framework.
This ensures that Directors remain
well equipped to discharge their
governance responsibilities efficiently.

Induction and
Familiarisation
Newly appointed Directors undergo
a comprehensive orientation
programme that includes interactive
sessions with Senior Management.
These engagements provide insights
into your Company’s operations,
financial performance, and strategic
roadmap. They also cover governance
responsibilities and explain how the
Board and its Committees function.
This structured approach enables
Directors to integrate seamlessly and
contribute meaningfully from the
outset.

Ongoing Learning and
Capability Enhancement
Beyond onboarding, the Board’s
learning journey is supported through
ongoing updates and thematic training
interventions aligned with evolving
governance priorities. This approach
extends beyond onboarding and
ensures continuous capability building
for Directors over time.
During the year, 100% of the Board
participated in specialised training
programmes covering key thematic
areas. Directors accessed these
programmes through your Company’s
Gurukul e-learning platform hosting
curated training modules. Training
covered key topics on sustainability
and emerging trends/risks in
information security.

These trainings are designed to
strengthen understanding of the
‘True Value’ methodology and its
application in the comprehensive
economic, environmental, and societal
impact of your Company's portfolio.
Sessions further addressed cyber risks,
data protection requirements, and
digital resilience considerations.
To ensure continuous alignment with
your Company’s strategic priorities,
Directors receive a regular Board
Newsletter throughout the year.
This publication keeps the Board
well-connected with the operations
of your Company by covering key
developments across your Company,
including thought leadership, product
updates, departmental updates, and
progress on key sustainability and CSR
initiatives.

Directors further engage with business
operations through on-ground
familiarisation visits. These visits provide
a deeper understanding of your
Company’s operating model, partner
ecosystem, and customer engagement.

Experiential and On-Ground Exposure
To deepen strategic insight, Directors engage with business operations through
branch visits, dealer interactions, and CSR project visits. These engagements enable
a closer understanding of customer needs, partner ecosystems, and operational
realities. This strengthens the linkage between Board-level oversight and executionlevel insights, further enhancing organisational alignment consistently and
effectively.

Board-Management
Engagement
Your Company fosters open and
consistent communication between the
Board and Senior Management through
both formal meetings and informal
interactions. These engagements
enable Directors to provide guidance,
share expertise, and align closely with
Management on strategic matters.
To ensure high-quality deliberation,
LTF’s Company Secretary ensures
that agenda materials are structured,
comprehensive, and circulated well in
advance. This commitment to timely
information-sharing supports focused
discussions and upholds its standards for
transparent and efficient governance.

Board Committees
The Board Committees operate under
clearly defined terms of reference
and support the Board in discharging
its fiduciary responsibilities. These
Committees are a core component
of your Company’s governance
framework, enabling focused oversight
across critical functional areas.

Key features of the
committee framework
include:
Committees constituted in
accordance with the Companies
Act, 2013, SEBI Listing Regulations,
and applicable RBI directives

Monitoring of fraud and defaults
Sustainability initiatives
While Committees provide specialised
focus across critical areas, they
operate within an integrated
governance architecture. Matters
assessed at Committee levels are
systematically escalated to the Board
for further review and approval, as
appropriate. This structure ensures that
accountability remains centralised and
decision-making is not fragmented.

Committee leadership and
composition framework:

Clearly defined charters outlining
roles, responsibilities, and authority
Periodic meetings
Structured reporting and
recommendation mechanisms to
the Board

These internal committees
and functional groups

of governance through

Operational and enterprise risks

Executive Officer, in line with RBI
requirements
All other Board Committees are

ensuring independent oversight
and objective decision-making

Communication
of Material
Information to the
Board
Your Company maintains a structured
escalation framework to ensure that
significant concerns relating to financial
integrity, regulatory compliance,
operational risks, ethical conduct, or
stakeholder interests are communicated
to the highest governance body in a
timely manner.
Such matters may arise through internal
audits, compliance testing programmes,
whistleblower reports, risk management
reviews, regulatory observations, or
management oversight processes.
These are escalated through defined
reporting channels to the relevant
Board Committees, including the Audit
Committee and Risk Management
Committee, and, as required, to the
Board.

The Secretarial Audit of your Company is
conducted annually in accordance with
the provisions of the Companies Act,
2013 and applicable SEBI regulations.
The Secretarial Auditor provides an
independent assessment of compliance
with corporate laws, governance
standards, and regulatory requirements.
To support systematic implementation
and stewardship, your Company follows
a structured three-tier compliance
architecture.
The report for FY26 has no observations or
adverse remarks.

Three-tier
Compliance
Architecture
The Secretarial Audit Report is
reviewed by the Board and forms part
of the statutory disclosures in the
Annual Report, which includes:
Business compliance teams
supporting implementation and
gap closure
Independent compliance testing
under an approved annual plan
Strengthened regional compliance
presence to ensure last-mile
adherence
This architecture supports timely
corrective action and reduction in
repeat observations across locations.

Integrating
Learnings into
Preventive
Controls
Learnings from fraud reviews, wilful
default assessments, and regulatory
observations are examined through
designated governance forums and
translated into enterprise-wide control
enhancements, which enable:
Centralised action tracking with
defined ownership
Periodic validation of control
adequacy through re-testing
Continuous strengthening of
preventive risk mechanisms

Governance over
Digital, AI and
Emerging Risks
As digital and analytics-driven models
expand, governance over technologyenabled processes has been
strengthened through:
Dedicated Data & AI Office,
strengthening governance over
data-driven decision-making
Defined control frameworks for
AI-enabled credit journeys
Review mechanisms to monitor
model drift, bias, and explainability
Enhanced governance over data
privacy and cybersecurity risks
Cyber and data privacy training for
frontline and customer-facing teams
is supplemented with effectiveness
assessments, rather than mere
completion metrics and risk awareness.

Vigil Mechanism
LTF’s Vigil Mechanism Framework enables employees, Directors, and external stakeholders to report concerns relating to
unethical conduct in a safe and confidential manner. Multiple secure reporting channels are available, including email,
written communication, and oral reporting, with safeguards to protect the whistleblower’s identity.
Reported matters are monitored by the Whistleblower Investigation Committee, which ensures objective assessment,
timely resolution, and appropriate corrective action. Through this mechanism, your Company reinforces ethical conduct,
accountability, and transparency across its operations.
For detailed policy access, refer to section ‘Policy Compendium’.

Policy on Prevention of Sexual Harassment at
the Workplace (POSH)
LTF is committed to maintaining a respectful and secure work environment. The
Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) Policy, aligns with the Sexual Harassment
of Women at Workplace Act, 2013, and extends to all employees, including
temporary and contract staff.
An Internal Committee, comprising internal and one external independent
member, is responsible for addressing complaints fairly. Training modules,
including onboarding and annual sensitisation, are delivered through your
Company’s in-house learning portal, Gurukul.
For further details on LTF’s Policy on POSH and incidences of sexual harassment,
refer to 'BRSR Section C: Principle 5'.

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

Policy on Related Party
Transactions (RPT)
LTF follows a robust governance
framework to ensure that all Related
Party Transactions (RPTs) are conducted
transparently and in the ordinary
course of business at arm’s length.
In line with SEBI Listing Regulations
and your Company’s Related Party
Transactions Policy, all RPTs are
reviewed and approved by the
Independent Directors, who are
members of the Audit Committee.
Directors who have a direct or indirect
interest in a transaction are required to
disclose such interest and abstain from
participating in discussions and voting
on the relevant agenda item. This
ensures that decisions relating to RPTs
are evaluated independently and in the
best interests of your Company and its
stakeholders.
The Statutory Auditors reviews
RPTs as part of the statutory audit
process. This ensures compliance with
applicable regulatory and relevant
accounting standards.

100%

Statutory Reports

It clearly defines bribery, governs
charitable donations, and restricts
political contributions, unless legally
permissible.
This policy applies to employees,
Directors, and third parties
representing LTF. A dedicated Risk
Containment Unit (RCU), monitors
and mitigates risks related to financial
misconduct and corruption.
Policy details are available in the 'Policy
Compendium' section.
For further details on LTF’s AntiCorruption Policy and incidences of
corruption in the system, refer to
'BRSR Section C: Principle 1'.

Sustainability Governance

To extend its ethical values across the value chain, LTF has established a ThirdParty Code of Conduct. This applies to vendors, borrowers, and business
associates, setting expectations for responsible conduct, legal compliance,
environmental stewardship, and social responsibility.

While sustainability stewardship is embedded across Board processes, learning initiatives and performance evaluation
mechanisms, this section outlines LTF’s dedicated sustainability governance framework designed to ensure focused
accountability and measurable progress.

Dividend Distribution Policy
The Dividend Distribution Policy is designed to strike a balance between
rewarding shareholders and maintaining financial flexibility. Dividend decisions
are based on profitability, retained earnings, liquidity, expansion plans, and
regulatory guidelines.
The Board examines these parameters periodically to ensure compliance
while delivering sustained value to shareholders. This disciplined approach
underscores LTF’s commitment to long-term growth and capital prudence.
For more information, refer to section 'Policy Compendium'.

LTF has established a robust, multi-tiered sustainability governance framework to ensure that environmental, social, and
governance priorities are embedded into every aspect of its operations. This framework enables clear accountability,
measurable progress, and strategic alignment with long-term goals and evolving regulatory requirements.

Sustainable Finance Framework, and maintains rigorous internal governance over product screening and
manages the green and social tagging processes essential for directing capital toward sustainable development.
	Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Council: Comprising of senior business leaders and functional heads, this
council elevates DEI from a standard initiative to a core business outcome owned by leadership. It provides
strategic direction and ensures inclusive practices are woven into your company’s operational and management
accountability.
	Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) Working Group: A dedicated group ensuring workplace safety
and regulatory compliance for health and safety practices. This group integrates expertise from facilities
management and the sustainability team and drives a proactive safety culture to manage system-driven
frameworks across entire branch network.

Corporate Overview

Value Chain Governance
LTF extends its governance framework beyond internal operations to its value
chain, ensuring that ethical conduct, compliance discipline, and responsible
business practices are consistently applied across vendors, borrowers, and business
associates.
A key element of this framework is the Third-Party Code of Conduct, which
governs interactions with vendors, borrowers, and business associates. This code
requires adherence to the highest legal and ethical benchmarks, including:
Compliance with all applicable laws
Prevention of conflicts of interest
Zero tolerance for bribery and corruption
Commitment to anti-trust regulations and data privacy protection
Implementation of strong business continuity strategies
Beyond compliance, value chain partners are encouraged to adopt practices
that reduce environmental impact. They are encouraged to adopt practices to
monitor greenhouse gas emissions and minimise waste. This approach reinforces
sustainability across the network.
Ethical employment standards form an integral part of value chain governance.
This includes fair wages, prohibition of forced and child labour, and provision
of safe working conditions. Partners are also encouraged to adopt sustainable
sourcing practices and support inclusive engagement with marginalised
communities.

Governance Driving
Strategic Value
Your Company’s governance goes
beyond compliance to act as a
strategic driver of sustainable growth
and institutional resilience. This
approach strengthens long-term
institutional stability and decisionmaking discipline.
In FY26, your Company strengthened
its governance architecture further.
It enhanced key policies, committee
charters, and authority matrices. These
improvements ensured greater clarity
in roles, accountability, and decision
rights. As a result, governance clarity
improved across the organisation.

The governance framework continued
to mature during the year. It showed
stronger alignment between
Board oversight and management
execution. This alignment enables
agile and informed decisionmaking in a dynamic operating
environment. The Board and its
Committees continued to play a
pivotal role in providing strategic
guidance and robust oversight.
The effectiveness of the Board was
further reinforced through structured
evaluation processes and continuous
engagement, enabling high-quality
governance.
Your Company also strengthened
its focus on ethics, compliance, and
integrity. It reinforced a culture of
responsible business conduct. The
Code of Conduct and whistleblower
mechanisms remained effective
in promoting transparency and
accountability, with concerns
resolved in a timely manner. Your
Company maintained a zerotolerance towards unethical practices.
Compliance frameworks were further
strengthened to ensure adherence to
evolving regulatory requirements.

Significant progress was made in
advancing ESG governance during
FY26. LTF institutionalised a robust
Environmental and Social Management
System (ESMS). This system enables
a structured approach to identifying,
assessing, and managing environmental
and social risks and impacts.
Your Company further advanced
transparency and disclosure standards.
Reporting depth improved in line with
sustainability framework requirements.
In addition, your Company was
recognised by the Institute of Company
Secretaries of India (ICSI) for its Business
Responsibility Sustainability Reporting
(BRSR). Data governance practices were
also strengthened to ensure accurate,
consistent, and timely disclosures,
thereby reinforcing stakeholder
confidence.
Your Company continues to maintain
high governance and ethical standards.
It has built a strong stakeholder
trust over a period of time through
consistent actions. This foundation
supports navigation of an increasingly
complex and evolving business
landscape, continuing to deliver
inclusive and sustainable growth.

Risk Management

RISK AS STRATEGIC
VALUE ENABLER
At LTF, risk
management goes
beyond compliance and
functions as a forwardlooking driver of strategic
growth. Your Company
embeds risk-first thinking
across operations to
support expansions in
an increasingly digital
ecosystem. This approach
is reinforced by a wellcalibrated strategic risk
posture. As a result, risk
management actively
enables sustainable and
responsible growth.

74

LTF transforms potential vulnerabilities
into strategic opportunities through
structured risk intelligence and
governance systems. It integrates
risk insights into decision-making
frameworks to strengthen longterm alignment and resilience. This
integration ensures that growth
remains controlled, scalable, and
aligned with broader business
objectives. Consequently, risk
management supports both stability
and strategic direction.
Proactive risk mitigation continues to
drive the strength of your Company’s
digital lending ecosystem. LTF uses
advanced AI-driven analytics to
deliver precise and efficient credit
underwriting. It combines multi-bureau
data, account aggregator insights, and
alternative trust signals to enhance
decision accuracy. This approach
enhances both speed and reliability in
credit assessments.
This seamless integration of
intelligent risk controls with digital
architecture enables your Company
to scale inclusive financial solutions
into emerging geographies while
maintaining uncompromising
asset quality and superior portfolio
performance.

Enabling Risk
Discipline
In an evolving financial ecosystem,
robust risk management is critical to
institutional stability and sustainable
growth. Your Company’s risk
management functions as a strategic
discipline, enabling informed risktaking while safeguarding stakeholder
interests. As business models scale,
technologies evolve, and regulatory
expectations intensify, the ability to
anticipate and manage uncertainty
becomes essential. To this end, by
embedding risk awareness across
governance structures, operational
processes, and strategic decisionmaking, your Company ensures that
growth remains responsible, resilient,
and aligned with its objectives.

A Multi-Tiered
Framework for
Strategic Oversight
LTF operates in a complex risk
environment shaped by interlinked
drivers, including macroeconomic
volatility, interest rate movements,
climate variability, digital disruption,
regulatory evolution, and changing
customer behaviour.

Liquidity adequacy and funding
stability
Market volatility and interest rate
movements
Operational vulnerabilities arising
from people, processes, and systems
Cybersecurity threats and technology
dependencies
Climate-related risks affecting asset
quality and regional exposures
Regulatory, geopolitical and
reputational developments
This anticipatory assessment enables early
recognition of potential stress points,
allowing the organisation to respond
proactively instead of reactively.
Anticipation is translated into action
through a structured, enterprise-wide risk
management framework that integrates
governance, analytics, technology and
people.
The framework is designed to ensure that
risks are:
Identified early
Assessed comprehensively
Prioritised based on impact and
likelihood
Mitigated through timely and
proportionate actions
By aligning risk responses with strategic
objectives, LTF reinforces consistency
between growth ambitions and prudence.

A Strategic and
Forward-looking Risk
Framework
LTF has established a robust
enterprise-wide risk framework that
enhances visibility across business
verticals, functions and geographies.
The framework integrates:
Quantitative models and
statistical tools
Qualitative management
judgment and sector insights
Portfolio-level analytics and trend
monitoring
Scenario analysis and stresstesting methodologies
Together, these elements provide
a holistic understanding of the risk
landscape and support informed
prioritisation.
To strengthen balance sheet
resilience, LTF conducts periodic stress
testing with a focus on liquidity and
interest rate risks. It actively monitors
interest rate sensitivity to maintain a
positive gap over a one-year horizon.
In parallel, liquidity stress testing is
undertaken under multiple adverse
scenarios to assess funding stability.
Your Company also maintains
adequate high-quality liquid assets
to ensure a minimum 30-day survival
period.

Identification and
Preparedness

timely identification of deteriorating
trends and early intervention.

Corporate Overview

Risk Appetite

Process Governance
Through the Product
& Process Approval
Group (PPAG)

Your Company’s risk management
is anchored in a clearly defined
Risk Appetite that establishes the
boundaries within which growth
is pursued. It provides a structured
articulation of the level and types of
risks LTF is willing to undertake. These
risk levels remain closely aligned with
strategic objectives and the evolving
operating environment.
The Risk Appetite is calibrated across
key dimensions of resilience, including
capital adequacy, liquidity, earnings
stability and franchise protection. The
framework integrates both quantitative
thresholds and qualitative guardrails
for effective risk control. These
parameters cover core risk areas as well
as emerging risks such as cybersecurity,
climate risk, and reputational
considerations.
To ensure effective implementation,
LTF cascades the Risk Appetite across
businesses through structured limits.
These controls include sectoral
exposures, regional concentrations,
unsecured portfolio thresholds,
and management-level limits. Such
structured controls strengthen
accountability and enhance enterprisewide risk discipline.

As operational complexity increases,
structured process governance ensures
standardisation and disciplined
execution of changes.
The Product and Process Approval
Group (PPAG) oversees:

Risk Management Tools and Systems
To enable a structured and forward-looking approach, your Company has
institutionalised a suite of risk management tools and digital enablers. These
tools strengthen the identification, assessment, and mitigation of risks across
the enterprise. As a result, LTF ensures more consistent and proactive risk
management practices.
At the core of this framework is a dynamic risk register, which provides a
consolidated view of key risks across businesses and functions. The register is
periodically updated and reviewed, capturing evolving risk scenarios, mitigation
actions, ownership, and timelines. This process ensures strong accountability, and
a timely response.

Operating in a dynamic and evolving business space, LTF employs a comprehensive risk management framework.
This framework addresses a wide range of potential risks across operational and strategic areas. It enables systematic
identification, assessment, and mitigation of threats through a structured and consistent approach. As a result, your
Company can respond effectively to emerging challenges.
The framework integrates strong governance practices, data-driven analytics, and proactive control measures. These
elements collectively enhance organisational resilience and support informed decision-making. In addition, they help
safeguard business continuity. This approach ensures that risk management remains aligned with overall business objectives.

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

Emerging Risks
Commodity Price
Volatility and Input
Cost Inflation
Persistent geopolitical friction continues
to drive unpredictable fluctuations in
global commodity and input prices.
We remain vigilant in evaluating the
sensitivity of our loan book to these
inflationary trends, employing rigorous
credit appraisal processes that account
for margin compression aiming to
mitigate the impact of input-price
shocks on our overall asset quality.

Model and Digital
Operational Risk
As we integrate advanced analytics into
our core operations, we face emerging
risks related to model deterioration
and implementation gaps. Emerging
risks include potential "black-box"
failures in Machine Learning (ML)
algorithms and algorithmic bias in
credit scoring, if such Machine Learning
algorithms use case increases. To
mitigate these, we have mandated
upfront validation for all new models
alongside the use of challenger models.
Our robust governance framework
includes quarterly model monitoring,

strengthened BRE implementation
testing under Digital Operational Risk
to safeguard our digital ecosystem.

Cyber Security and
Identity Risk
The digital frontier for financial
institutions is under constant threat,
with cyber-attacks historically
increasing during periods of global
conflict and domestic election cycles.
To counter these evolving threats,
we have transitioned toward a Zero
Trust Architecture and an "Identity
First" security approach. Our
defense strategy includes increased
frequency of vulnerability testing, the
implementation of DevSecOps, and
continuous monitoring through our
Risk Appetite Framework (RAF). By
closely tracking End of Life (EoL) and
End of Service (EoS) systems, we ensure
that our technological infrastructure
remains resilient against unauthorized
access and sophisticated external fraud

Integration across
Business Units
Risk management is deeply embedded
across all business units and functions
within your Company. This integration
ensures a unified and enterprise-wide

approach to identifying and managing
risks. As a result, risk awareness remains
central to daily operations and decisionmaking.
The Enterprise Risk Management
(ERM) framework enables consistent
identification, assessment, and
mitigation of risks. It applies across
products, geographies, and operational
functions within the organisation. Risk
considerations are integrated into:
Business strategy and planning
processes
Product and process approvals
(through governance bodies such as
PPAG)
Credit underwriting and portfolio
management
Operational workflows and decisionmaking systems
Your Company enables cross-functional
collaboration through integrated
committees, shared dashboards,
and standardised processes. These
ensure that risk insights are seamlessly
embedded into day-to-day operations.
This integrated approach ensures that
risk management acts as a strategic
enabler, supporting sustainable growth,
operational resilience, and informed
decision-making across the organisation.
