Fraud Risk Management
In an era of rapid financial digitisation, at LTF, we recognise that a resilient Fraud Risk Management (FRM) framework is not
merely a regulatory mandate but a fundamental pillar of our enterprise risk strategy. As an Upper Layer NBFC, your Company
has institutionalised a comprehensive FRM framework that is meticulously aligned with the RBI Master Directions. Your
Company's approach is predicated on the belief that safeguarding its financial integrity and the trust of stakeholders requires
a dynamic, "Zero Tolerance" stance against unethical practices.
This commitment is reflected in our multi-layered governance architecture, where accountability is driven from the top. LTF
has a dedicated Risk Containment Unit (RCU) entrusted with the responsibility of Fraud Risk Management and safeguarding
Company against financial frauds. The Senior Management oversees initiatives being taken by this unit through the Fraud
Risk Management Group (FRMG) which serves as the primary body for monitoring effectiveness of FRM framework. The
Special Committee of the Board (SCBMF) and the Audit Committee (ACB) provide rigorous quarterly oversight, scrutinising
root causes and enforcing staff accountability to ensure that the defensive posture remains impenetrable
At LTF, Fraud Risk Management is operationalised through 4 pillars:

Hi I am

Sachet Kumar

I

II

86

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

Prevention

Detection and Investigation

III

Reporting and
remedial actions

IV

Communication and
Awareness Building

We have fortified the first pillar:
Prevention, by embedding real-time,
multi-factor verification protocols
and dynamic data analytics into the
customer onboarding journey. By
identifying discrepancies at the point of
entry, LTF effectively neutralise threats
prior to fund disbursement.
To address the evolving nature of
sophisticated financial crimes, the
second pillar is critical wherein detection
capabilities are powered by an advanced
Early Warning Signals (EWS) framework.
This system is deeply integrated
with your Company's central data
warehouse, allowing for the continuous
monitoring of portfolio behavioural
patterns to flag latent risks. When
anomalies are detected, the RCU takes
immediate action, overseeing the endto-end lifecycle of investigation to fraud
detection to the recovery process.
The third pillar ensures that your
Company remains fully compliant to
all directives issued by Reserve Bank
of India (RBI) with regards to Fraud
reporting including all mandatory
disclosures, reportings and remedial

actions. Beyond compliance, this
framework facilitates swift remedial
actions, where investigative findings
are translated into immediate
systemic enhancements to fortify the
defenses against evolving financial
crimes.
Through the fourth Pillar
communication and awareness
building, LTF fosters a pervasive
risk-awareness culture through the
persona of our mascot, “Sachet
Kumar,” who empowers employees
to act as an informed first line of
defense. Through engaging "Sachet
Kumar" campaigns, the mascot
simplifies complex fraud threats
like phishing and identity theft into
relatable advice, positioning your
Company as a proactive partner in
customer security and ensuring its
customers remain equally informed
and protected against evolving
financial threats. LTF drives this
commitment through customised risk
awareness series, specialised training
for sales and operations, reward
programs like the “Sachet Awards”
to promote and encourage people

for identifying unique and innovative
fraud detection and prevention ideas.
To further strengthen awareness
building, your Company conducted
'Fraud Awareness Week' in the
month of Oct 2025 by engaging
2000+ employees through knowledge
series practical learning bites, special
webinars and expert sessions on digital
fraud trends through industry experts.
By integrating these rigorous
internal controls with a transparent
reporting mechanism, LTF continues
to demonstrate its unwavering
commitment to ethical excellence and
long-term value preservation.

Macroeconomic Review
The global economy in 2025 navigated
a landscape defined by divergent
growth paths and a fundamental
reordering of trade priorities. The most
significant disruptor in 2025 was the
implementation of tariff rates climb to
levels not seen in a century, triggering
a wave of global "front-loading"
where businesses rushed to import
goods early in the year. Nevertheless,
global growth is estimated to have
slowed to ~2.8-3.0% in 2025, down
from the 3.3% seen in 2024. This
slowdown, however, was not uniform
across major economies. In the US,
growth decelerated to 1.8% as policy
uncertainty and the drag from higher
input costs began to weigh on business
environment. China’s economic growth
slipped toward 4%, hampered by a

stagnant property sector and the
adverse impact of trade barriers from
the West. The Eurozone struggled
with a meager 0.8-1.2% growth rate,
particularly as Germany faced structural
industrial challenges and fluctuating
energy costs.

England (BoE) moved aggressively with
rate cuts as their economies cooled. In
a historic shift, the Bank of Japan (BoJ)
continued to raise rates toward 0.5%
to manage domestic price pressures
and a weakening Yen, ending its era of
ultra-loose policy.

Amid the growth pangs, the
disinflationary process that hit a
milestone in 2024 became sticky and
desynchronised in 2025. This led to
divergences in monetary policy actions
of major developed economies. The
US Federal Reserve, after holding rates
steady for much of the year due to
tariff-induced inflation risks, finally
initiated a series of 25 basis points
cuts in late 2025 as the labor market
showed signs of cooling. The European
Central Bank (ECB) and Bank of

Despite a global backdrop of trade
fragmentation, the Indian economy
demonstrated remarkable resilience
and structural strength through
FY26 supported by buoyant private
consumption, robust manufacturing
sector, and strategic government
capital expenditure. It remained the
world's fastest-growing major economy
for the fourth consecutive year and
surpassed Japan in nominal GDP to
become the world's fourth-largest
economy.

89

Corporate Overview

According to the second advance
estimates, India’s real GDP growth for
FY26 is estimated at 7.6%, surpassing
previous projections. This growth is
anchored in a robust manufacturing
performance and resilient domestic
demand despite external headwinds
like geopolitical tensions in West Asia
and tariff concerns. The economy
showed strong momentum through
the first three quarters of FY26, with
a slight moderation expected in the
final quarter due to the Middle East
conflict and an unfavourable statistical
base. The manufacturing sector
maintained double-digit growth for
five consecutive quarters as of Q3FY26,
bolstered by inventory stocking and GST
rationalisation. Services sector reached a
seven-quarter high of 9.5% in Q3FY26.
Growth was led by Financial, Real Estate
& Professional Services and Trade,
Hotels, and Transport. Growth in the
Agriculture and allied sector moderated
to a seven-quarter low of 1.4% in
Q3FY26, partly due to unfavorable base
effects and weather disruptions like
unseasonal rainfall.
Headline inflation plummeted to an
average of ~2.1% for FY26
(vs. 4.6% in FY25), driven by a
prolonged nine-month decline in food
prices and a favorable monsoon. Core
inflation (excluding gold) remained
stable at around 2.6%, indicating that
the underlying price pressures were
well-contained. The RBI delivered
one of the sharpest easing cycles in
recent history, cutting policy rates by
a full percentage point within four
months starting in February, 2025.
The RBI delivered another rate cut of
25 basis points in December, 2025,
even as macroeconomic fundamentals
strengthened, signalling its commitment
to sustain domestic demand through
2026 amid global uncertainty.

90

On the fiscal front, the Central
Government is set to narrow its
fiscal gap to 4.4% of GDP for FY26
compared to 4.8% in FY25. During
Apr-Jan FY26, fiscal deficit stood at
₹9.8 lakh Cr, representing 63% of
the budget estimate. Significant tax
reforms in FY26 (personal income tax
revisions and GST rationalisation) had
a revenue-reducing effect, with an
estimated tax revenue shortfall of
₹1.9 lakh Cr in FY26 (RE), which was
balanced by higher non-tax revenue
and expenditure adjustments.
Foreign direct investment (FDI) into
India grew by 18% year-on-year to
US$47.87 Bn during April-December,
FY26, with inflows from the US nearly
doubling to US$7.8 Bn during this
period. Foreign portfolio investment
(FPI) outflows were about US$16.4 Bn
(up to March 25, 2026), highest ever
in any financial year. Such investment
withdrawals contributed significantly to
currency depreciation with the Indian
Rupee breaching the ₹94 per US dollar
mark in late March 2026. Despite RBI’s
interventions in the forex market to
contain rupee volatility, India’s foreign
exchange reserves at US$710 Bn are
adequate to cover for more than 11
months of imports.
In 2025, S&P upgraded India’s
long-term sovereign credit rating to
‘BBB’ from ‘BBB-’ and its short-term
rating to ‘A-2’ from ‘A-3’, with a Stable
Outlook. This marked the country’s
first sovereign upgrade by S&P in
18 years. The rating upgrade reflects a
combination of key factors, including
India’s buoyant and dynamic economic
growth, the government’s sustained
commitment to fiscal consolidation,
improved quality of public spending,
particularly on capex and infrastructure,
and strong corporate, financial, and
external balance sheets. Credible

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

inflation management and increasing
policy predictability have also played a
central role.
The GST 2.0 reform, implemented in
September 2025, represents a landmark
shift by aggressively streamlining
India’s once-complex rate structure.
By collapsing the 12% and 28%
brackets into more manageable 5%
and 18% tiers, the GST Council has
effectively lowered the cost of essential
services, life-saving medicines, and
middle-class consumer durables. While
the introduction of a consolidated
40% peak rate for luxury and "sin"
goods ensures revenue stability, the
broader exemption of health and life
insurance premiums highlights a pivot
toward social welfare. Early data from
2026 suggests that this rationalisation
has successfully balanced the scales:
reducing the compliance burden for
small businesses and cooling inflation,
while simultaneously driving
record-high monthly collections through
increased volume.
The much-awaited Labour Code
reforms finally came into force in
FY26. By simplifying compliance while
safeguarding worker rights, the reform
is expected to improve ease of doing
business, accelerate job formalisation,
and attract fresh investment across
manufacturing and services.
While domestic reforms progressed,
FY26 was defined by a sophisticated
shift in India’s external economic
strategy. The year marked a pivot
toward deeper global integration
through the conclusion of landmark
trade agreements with the US, EU, UK,
New Zealand, and Oman. These trade
pacts materially expand market access,
diversifying India’s trade corridors and
catalyzing new investment flows and
export opportunities.

Statutory Reports

Financial Statements

Outlook for FY27
The global economy had been on course for stronger-than-expected growth before the war in Iran erupted in the end of
February 2026. The uncertainty surrounding the duration and magnitude of the war is testing the resilience of the global
economy. The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has warned that escalating conflict in the
Middle East has knocked the ‌global economy off a stronger growth path as a near-halt in energy shipments through the
Strait of Hormuz threatens to push inflation sharply higher. It projects global GDP growth to ease from 3.3% last year to
2.9% in 2026 as the unpredictable nature of the conflict offset tailwinds from strong technology-related investment, lower
effective tariff rates and momentum carried over from 2025.
S&P Global Ratings projects India’s GDP to grow at 7.1% in FY27 driven by strong private consumption, modest recovery in
private investment and robust export performance. However, downside risks to the outlook could emanate from the Gulf
conflict. It projects inflation to rise to 4.3% in FY27 as it normalises from low levels, with the RBI likely to keep interest rates
unchanged while maintaining a neutral stance.

IMF

World Bank

Projections for India's GDP
Growth in FY27

India’s financial system recorded steady credit expansion, low asset quality stress and evolving regulatory measures in FY26.
The incremental bank credit growth for FY26 is estimated at ~₹26 Tn, ~14.5% Y-o-Y expansion.
As per the RBI's December 2025 Financial Stability Report, the Indian banking system is robust and resilient, boasting multidecadal low Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) ratios of 2.1%. Capital buffers are strong, with Commercial Banks
well-positioned to handle financial shocks, though risks from surging unsecured retail loans and fintech lending require
caution. Similarly, the RBI sees the NBFC sector as being stable, showing strong capital buffers, robust earnings, and
improving asset quality.
While the overall health of banks and NBFCs are robust, the RBI has flagged specific areas that require constant vigilance.
First, unsecured loans (personal loans, credit cards) accounted for 53.1% of all retail loan slippages. The RBI noted that
private banks are particularly exposed, with 76% of their fresh slippages coming from this segment. Secondly, high
impairment has been observed in borrowers who have unsecured loans from five or more lenders, a trend driven largely by
rapid fintech credit expansion (36.1% Y-o-Y growth). Furthermore, while domestic fundamentals are strong, external risks
such as, geopolitical tensions, trade uncertainty, and Rupee volatility remain elevated.
According to CRIF Highmark, India’s retail lending market surged to ₹162.7 lakh Cr by December 2025, supported by
690 Mn active accounts. The market is undergoing a clear structural shift: secured products like gold and auto loans are
now significantly outperforming unsecured segments. While rising asset values and favorable LTV ratios are boosting ticket
sizes in top-tier categories, the real volume story is happening in Beyond Top 100 (BT-100) cities. Driven by robust digital
infrastructure, these Tier 2 and Tier 3 markets have become the primary engines for personal loans, two-wheelers, and
consumer durables.

Financial Statements

FY26 Business Performance
Retail Finance

Farmer Finance
The Farmer Finance vertical includes
offerings across the farmer lifecycle.

Farm Equipment Finance
In FY26, LTF delivered a benchmarksetting performance in the Farm
Equipment Finance business,
effectively leveraging a highly
favourable rural macroeconomic
environment. Strong monsoons,
healthy reservoir levels, robust Kharif
and Rabi harvests, and timely mandi
arrivals significantly strengthened
farmer liquidity, creating a conducive
environment.
A major external catalyst was the
government’s strategic reduction of
GST on tractors from 18% to 5%,
which sharply lowered acquisition
costs and accelerated mechanisation
demand nationwide. The tractor
industry consequently grew by
23%, expanding from ~9.4 lakh to
~11.6 lakh units. Your Company
responded with agility, aligning
distribution, underwriting, and
digital capabilities to fully capture
this upswing.

Rural Business Finance
LTF financed ~1.11 lakh units in FY26
(vs. ~95,000 in FY25), delivering
disbursements of ~₹6,200 Cr excluding
financing to existing customers which
contributed a robust 22% to overall
disbursements, total disbursements
reached ~₹7,900 Cr, resulting in 11%
book growth leading to a book-size of
~₹16,600 Cr. Additionally, collection
efficiency enhanced by 110 bps,
underlining continued strengthening of
asset quality.
Project Cyclops, now fully implemented,
has significantly strengthened your
Company’s new-age credit underwriting
architecture. The platform integrates
real-time land record retrieval, geospatial
and hyperlocal data sets, decilebased customer profiling, video PD,
account aggregator data, and seamless
e-mandate registration. Together, these
enhancements have sharpened risk
discrimination, enabled a fully digitised
employee-assisted sourcing journey, and
delivered industry-leading turnaround
times (TAT) across the underwriting
process.

Dealer engagement and operational
utility were further strengthened through
the launch of PARTNER PLANET, a
dedicated digital platform designed to
enhance the effectiveness and efficiency
of dealer interactions. The application
provides real-time visibility into trade
advances and incorporates comprehensive
business management functionalities,
enabling improved planning, tracking,
and decision-making. By delivering a
structured, high-utility engagement
infrastructure, this initiative is expected to
reinforce dealer relationships and support
sustained improvements in throughput
over the medium to long term.
Looking ahead to FY27, asset quality is
expected to further improve as the Project
Cyclops underwritten book seasons,
complemented by strong demand
momentum and continued digital
innovation.

Warehouse Receipting Finance
In the Warehouse Receipting Finance
business, your Company continued to
scale its presence in the agricultural value
chain through a fully digital, paperless
process with API integration into
collateral managers. A rule-based digital
MTM mechanism further strengthened
risk monitoring. The segment delivered
sanctions of ~₹1,700 Cr and disbursals
of ~₹800 Cr, establishing a strong
competitive position in post-harvest
financing.
Overall, FY26 marks a year of disciplined
execution and digital leadership,
positioning your Company strongly for
sustained expansion in FY27.

96

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

FY26 was a year of fortitude for
Rural Group Loans & Microfinance
franchise of LTF. Your Company was
able to maintain the growth and
portfolio quality despite industry
wide overleveraging cycle. Due to
LTF’s prudent sourcing and portfolio
monitoring mechanisms, your
Company was able to maintain one
of the lowest credit costs across
the industry. Notably, LTF also
effectively navigated the fallout
of the Karnataka Micro Loan and
Small Loan Ordinance, 2025 by
Q3FY26, emerging far ahead of
the competition due to resilient
collection efforts coupled with
on-field discipline and customer
communication.

Despite a broader microfinance sector
contraction where industry portfolio
de-grew by 16% from ₹3.8 lakh Cr to
₹3.2 lakh Cr and the active customer
base shrank from 8.9 Cr to 7.7 Cr, your
Company defied the trend. LTF thus
recorded a healthy growth to ₹29,367
Cr from ₹25,838 Cr in FY25 a robust
14% increase. This was backed by
stringent sourcing guardrails and data
backed underwriting. This disciplined

approach resulted in a sustainable highquality portfolio where 98.5% of the
loan book consists of customers with
less than 3 lenders, 53% of whom are
exclusive to LTF. This has translated into
one of the industry’s best portfolios
with collection efficiency of 99.8% and
a 0-DPD book of 96.4%.

Property (Micro LAP) as a strategic
high margin, secured growth engine.
Over the past two years, the product
has effectively addressed the financing
requirements of rural segments by
leveraging localised geo expertise, robust
underwriting capabilities and data led
customer insights.

Heading into FY27, LTF intends to grow
by expanding into under penetrated
geographies. In the second half of
FY27, your Company will further
strengthen its portfolio monitoring
with help of data analytics as it deploys
Project Nostradamus, an advanced
portfolio management engine. This will
enable LTF to monitor early warning
signals, customer location stability,
customer leverage and other geo and
demographic signals within the rural
business finance segment.

In FY26, Micro LAP surpassed the
loan book of ₹1,400 Cr milestone,
a significant leap from ₹482 Cr in
FY25. This momentum was driven by a
granular network of over 260 branches,
with existing branches in Tamil Nadu,
Karnataka, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra
Pradesh, and Telangana and foray into
high-opportunity markets of Rajasthan,
Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh.

accelerate this trajectory by deepening its

a cornerstone of the Rural Business
Finance portfolio, leveraging localised
insights to drive sustainable, secured
portfolio growth.

SME Finance

Personal Loans
The Personal Loan industry aggregates
to a market size of ~₹15.9 lakh Cr for
FY26 with a Y-o-Y industry growth of
11%, led by disbursements in high
ticket size segments.
During the year, LTF successfully scaled
its Personal Loans portfolio across all
primary channels, including
cross-sell initiatives, Large Partnerships,
and Direct Selling Agents (DSAs).
The strategic focus remained on
driving risk-calibrated growth while
engineering a tech-led, profitable
consumer lending business dedicated
to delivering a superior customer
experience.
With ‘data and technology-first’
approach to lending, the business
was built through LTF’s proprietary

underwriting engine – Project Cyclops,
partnership-led alternate data models
and rigorous portfolio monitoring. In
Personal Loans, all the originations are
digital in nature delivering superior
customer experience along with
enhanced risk controls.
These initiatives resulted in
disbursement of ~₹12,200 Cr in
FY26 with the book size crossing
₹14,600 Cr with a Y-o-Y growth of
70%. As the regulatory environment
evolves alongside rapid technological
advancements, your Company remains
well-positioned to navigate industry
shifts and maintain its upward growth
trajectory.

The Micro, Small, and Medium
Enterprises (MSME) sector continues
to be a cornerstone of India’s
economy, contributing significantly to
manufacturing, exports, and employment.
As of January 2026, as a part of the
details shared in Economic Survey
2025-2026, credit to MSMEs registered
a Y-o-Y growth of 8.8%, surpassing the
6.2% growth observed in credit to large
enterprises.
Among the factors that contributed
to LTF’s growth were comprehensive
expansion in 100+ locations in addition
to the existing 118 locations. Additionally,
a new program variant; partially
Collateralised Business Loans has been
introduced to meet evolving customer
needs that would enable smarter capital
structuring along with expansion & scale
up of Direct Sales Team.
Furthermore, Project Cyclops marked
a significant milestone in transforming
LTF’s credit assessment framework by
designing a scorecard to enable faster
risk assessment, improved customer
segmentation, and enhanced consistency
and accuracy in decision-making.
Alongside Project Cyclops was Project
Helios – an AI driven underwriting
co-pilot which was launched to enhance
the speed, accuracy, and efficiency of SME
credit decisions.
These initiatives contributed to the loan
book scaling to over ₹8,500 Cr in FY26
vs ~₹6,500 Cr in FY25. LTF disbursed
~₹6,100 Cr during the year, a 23%
increase from the previous year, with
the on-book customer base growing to
49,000+. The Union Budget 2025-26 has
further bolstered this sector by enhancing
the investment and turnover limits for
MSME classification by 2.5 and 2 times,
respectively, facilitating business expansion
and improved efficiency.

HUMAN RESOURCES

Gold Finance

LTF has significantly strengthened its
retail portfolio by its foray into Gold
Finance business – a secure high yield
product. This was engineered through
the acquisition of Paul Merchants
Finance’s gold loan business. This loan
book acquisition completed in June
2025, brought in over 130 branches,
approximately 700 employees, and
a book outstanding of ~₹1,300 Cr.
A notable highlight of this transaction
was that both technology and
personnel integration of the business
was completed within just eight weeks,
displaying high operational efficiency.
The Gold Finance vertical is now a core
part of LTF’s diversified retail franchise,
serving as a high-yield secured product.
LTF intends to scale this segment into
a key "fulcrum" product. LTF has since
added 200 new branches - focusing
on high-potential areas - bringing its
total distribution network to 330 gold
finance branches. Going forward, LTF
is poised to further expand on this
strong physical base backbone of
branches and grow its franchise. By
balancing its presence across both rural
and urban markets, your Company
aims to turn the Gold Finance business

Financial Statements

into a significant driver of long-term
retail growth.
Another key aspect and analogy to
the acquisition is the synergy that it
brings along – Cross-Sell potential.
It is to be noted that LTF’s existing
customer base in Rural Group
Loans (RGL) & Microfinance (MFI),
Farm Equipment, and Two-Wheeler
Finance indicate a high potential
for availing Gold finance due to its
already existing pertinent exposure
to Gold Finance. LTF is expected to
capitalise on this opportunity that
could, over the course of time, funnel
in sizeable amount of customers.
To drive lead generation and direct
it towards its branches, LTF is
leveraging its extensive rural network
of over 15,000 field employees.
All these efforts have yielded rich
results as LTF in a short period of
just 10 months, has displayed strong
disbursement of ₹6,700 Cr with loan
book increasing to over ₹2,800 Cr.
Going forward, the Gold Finance
product is expected to emerge as
the axis of RoA expansion to the LTF
product propositions.

With a stronger operating foundation
now in place, LTF is entering its next
phase of growth. As your Company
transitions from Lakshya 2026 to
Lakshya 2031, it is evolving into a
more data-driven, technology-led
organisation, with a clear ambition
to become India’s leading risk-first,
technology-first, multi-product retail
financier of choice.
At the heart of this evolution is a
clear focus on future-proofing the
organisation through conscious culture
change, aligning business priorities
closely with people practices. A strong
HR strategy remains central to this
effort, with capability building at its
core, alongside a continued focus
on creating a learning, caring and
performance-driven organisation.
Together, these efforts ensure that
talent, technology and culture work in
tandem to drive long-term value.
The underlying factor contributing
to the significant progress achieved
is the “People” of your Company,
who represent the L&T Finance brand
to diverse customer groups across
rural and urban areas, enabling
your Company to fulfil the financial
aspirations of Indians as a trusted
partner in every customer’s financial
journey. As of March 31, 2026, your
Company employs 41,301 employees
across three different categories:
The field force that comprises the
sales, collections, and operations
staff
The supervisory and middle
management personnel at the state
and zonal levels
The middle and top management
located at the corporate office

approximately form 71% of the
workforce composition and are
instrumental in delivering financial

Competency Framework:
To strengthen performance and

Competency Framework that connects
strategy to day-to-day behaviour. The
framework is structured around four

are evaluated for feasibility and impact, presented to senior leadership, recognised and rewarded, and integrated into live
workflows where viable.

and fostering enterprise-wide adoption of AI solutions. Through these initiatives, LTF democratises innovation, empowers
employees across all levels, and accelerates the organisation’s AI journey at scale.

Engaging Employees

Caring For Employees

LTF follows a structured annual plan for employee engagement, aligned with organisational goals and executed
collaboratively by the HR team with zonal and regional representatives.

A major step in strengthening
employee healthcare in FY26 was the
adoption of a corporate healthcare
platform, marking a shift from periodic
health interventions to a continuous,
preventive care model. The platform
enables employees to manage their
health proactively by providing access
to OPD consultations, concessional
medicines, automated follow-ups with
doctors and dieticians after health
checkups, and ongoing engagement
for lifestyle and preventive care. This
continuous care approach ensures
that health checkups translate into
sustained follow-up and improved
health outcomes, rather than
remaining standalone events.

A key milestone in FY26 was the launch of the one-stop engagement platform, designed to engage, recognise and
reward employees, while bringing together our geographically diverse workforce. With over 11,000 downloads, it
enables employees to seamlessly participate in initiatives, access benefits, and stay connected across the organisation.
Several organisation-wide programmes, including talent showcases, wellness challenges, and creative contests, have been
successfully conducted through the platform.

Sports and Wellness
The LTF Premier League (LPL) brought
employees together through cricket
competitions across 20 cities, featuring
281 teams with mandatory female
participation in each XI. Badminton
and Pickleball tournaments further
promoted inclusivity, teamwork, and
healthy competition. The selection
of the LTF Corporate Team provided
employees an opportunity to
represent the organisation externally.
International Yoga Day was celebrated
with desk yoga sessions and a
“Strike a Yoga Pose” contest, fostering
mindfulness and well-being.

Celebrating Creativity
and Talent

Platforms like LTF Got Talent Season,

to showcase artistic and intellectual
abilities. LTF Got Talent received 123
entries across singing, dancing, drama,
instrumental, and emcee categories,
while the photography contest
recorded 1,514 submissions, reflecting
strong participation and enthusiasm.

International Women’s Day 2026 was
celebrated under the theme “Give to
Gain,” recognising women employees
through workshops, interactive
sessions, and personalised gifts. LTF
also celebrated Independence Day,
Ganesh Chaturthi, Navratri, Diwali,
Christmas and harvest festivals
including Lohri, Bihu, Pongal, and

unity, and cultural pride across all

Employee Well-being and
Support
LTF continues to prioritise holistic

such as women-friendly policies
offering extended leave, work-fromhome options, childcare benefits,

structured financial, educational, and

Driving A Culture of
HR Excellence
In FY26, your Company participated in the CII HR Excellence Assessment
for the first time, a nationally recognised benchmark for evaluating people
practices and organisational effectiveness. The assessment involved a
comprehensive review of key HR processes, focusing on both execution and
measurable outcomes. It enabled structured identification of improvement
areas, adoption of best practices, and creation of a roadmap for continuous
enhancement. Your Company was felicitated with the recognition for
“Strong Commitment to HR Excellence,” reinforcing its focus on building a
culture of excellence in HR.

Employee Volunteering, Boondein
The Employee Volunteering Program – Boondein has grown into a high-impact initiative. A total of 1,000+ employees,
contributed 2,014 hours to social and environmental causes. Activities included recording audiobooks, creating braille books,
developing teaching materials, upcycling T-shirts into tote bags, and crafting eco-friendly bangles. The program is driven by a
strategic collaboration between HR and CSR teams to foster a culture of giving and community engagement.
Through these initiatives, LTF continues to foster a culture of participation, creativity, wellness, and inclusivity, connecting
employees across locations and building a vibrant, people-first workplace.

Corporate Overview

Risk Management
As we navigate the fiscal year 2026, LTF continues to fortify its position as a premier, tech-enabled Non-Banking Financial
Company (NBFC). LTF’s commitment to a "Right to Win" strategy is underpinned by a sophisticated Risk Management
Architecture that balances aggressive growth with systemic resilience. Your Company’s Risk First, Tech First approach
inculcates risk culture which directly contributes to long-term value creation, capital preservation, along with the
prestigious domestic AAA credit rating by all four domestic rating agencies and international rating BBB by S&P and
BBB- by Fitch (at par with Sovereign rating).

The Philosophy of Resilience: Enterprise Risk Management (ERM)
LTF views Risk Management not merely as a compliance requirement, but as a systematic approach to identifying,
evaluating, and mitigating potential and existing threats. Our Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Policy, reviewed annually
or as needed, serves as the structural blueprint for our risk-intelligent culture. This framework ensures that your Company
proactively address risks that could impede its strategic objectives.

Governance and Oversight

The Risk Appetite Statement (RAS)

The Board of Directors maintain ultimate
oversight through the Risk Management
Committee (RMC), which convenes quarterly
to monitor the implementation of your
Company’s risk frameworks. This committee
scrutinises risk tolerance, exposure limits, and
the efficacy of our early warning systems.

LTF’s strategic boundary is defined by the Risk Appetite Statement, a
comprehensive articulation of the risks your Company chooses to accept
or avoid to meet its business goals. This statement integrates both
qualitative and quantitative measures across four critical pillars:

In addition, there are frequent senior
management meetings where all emerging
and existing risks are discussed along with
appropriate strategies to mitigate the
same. Various internal meetings like Risk
Management Committee (RMC), Operational
Risk Management Committee (ORMC),
Outsourcing Management Committee
(OMC), Model Risk Management Committee
(MRMC), Reputational Risk Working
Group Committee (RRWG) and Asset
Liability Committee (ALCO) are conducted
on periodical intervals for assessing and
reviewing various aspects of risk arising from
the business operations of LTF.
Furthermore, a collaborative synergy
between your Company’s business units
and the risk department ensures that every
strategic decision is informed by real-time
risk data.

Managing operational scorecards, cybersecurity, and

To ensure these objectives are met at the granular level, LTF utilises the
Earnings at Risk (EAR) approach. This forward-looking metric allows LTF
to set concentration limits across all retail businesses, ensuring that any
potential loss remains within a predetermined, acceptable buffer.

Credit Risk: The
Vanguard of Digital
Underwriting
Credit risk, comprising transaction
(default) and portfolio (intrinsic and
concentration) risks, remains a focal
point of our management strategy.
LTF over the course of time has
transitioned to a pragmatic framework
that aligns every financing opportunity
with our risk-return strategy.

Project Cyclops:
AI-Driven Precision
The centerpiece of LTF's credit
evolution is Project Cyclops, it’s
proprietary AI-driven digital credit
engine. By leveraging advanced
machine learning and big data
analytics, Project Cyclops has
streamlined the entire credit lifecycle
from origination and underwriting to
continuous monitoring. This platform
integrates alternative data signals, such
as account aggregator data and bureau
insights, to enable:

Statutory Reports

advanced Early Warning Signals (EWS)
engine. Operating at both macro and
micro levels, Project Nostradamus
analyzes behavioral patterns and
macroeconomic variables to predict
defaults before they manifest. LTF’s
in-house economic affairs team tracks
multiple macro variables, issuing
regular bulletins to steer it’s portfolio
away from potential headwinds.
Further, your Company has established
benchmark vintage thresholds based
on budgeted credit costs to closely
monitor the portfolio and take timely
actions to navigate the portfolio
towards envisaged returns.

Strategic Portfolio
Reorientation
In it’s journey toward maximum
resilience, LTF is intentionally tilting
the portfolio toward secured products,
such as Gold Finance and Micro LAP.
This shift, combined with real-time
risk dashboards, ensures that your
Company's other retail products
including Rural Group Loans &
Microfinance, SME loans, and Farm
Equipment finance are monitored with
unprecedented granularity.

Swift Credit Decisioning
Enabling real-time evaluations
Prime Onboarding
Facilitating faster acquisition of
high-credit-quality customers
Strategic Sifting
Distinguishing between sensitive
and resilient borrowers to optimise
"swap-in/swap-out" rates

Project Nostradamus: Early
Warning Intelligence
To complement Project Cyclops, LTF
deployed Project Nostradamus, an

Operational and
Model Risk: Ensuring
Execution Excellence
Operational Risk is a “process and
people risk” that LTF manages
through rigorous internal controls.
The internal controls deployed utilises
a Risk and Control Matrix (RCM)
approach to identify and assess risks
across customer onboarding, business
operations, and collections.

Financial Statements

Process Governance
Every new product or feature
modification undergoes a strict approval
process via the Product and Process
Approval Group (PAG). To mitigate
"People Risk"—including human
error or fraud—the Operational Risk
department periodically updates
Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

Corrective Action &
Resilience
When risk events occur, LTF employs
Root Cause Analysis (RCA) to initiate
corrective steps. Your Company’s
Corrective and Preventive Action
(CAPA) plans are drafted with specific
timelines and are monitored by senior
leadership until closure. Furthermore,
LTF also has an enterprise-level non-IT
Business Continuity Plan (BCP) to ensure
operational stability during crises.

Model Risk Management
As LTF’s reliance on algorithms grows,
your Company has established a
dedicated Model Risk Management
framework. Models are used in areas
including credit risk management,
collection decisions, market risk
management, and financial reporting.
To govern model risk, a Board-approved
Model Risk Management policy and
Model Risk Management Committee
was put in place in FY24. Overseen by
a specialised committee, LTF reviews
models at every stage development,
active use, and retirement to prevent
inaccuracies in risk assessment. All
models within Project Cyclops and
Nostradamus are validated before
implementation and monitored
periodically.

Corporate Overview

Financial Statements

Sustainability
Technological
Fortification:
Cybersecurity &
Infosec
In an increasingly digital landscape,
protecting LTF’s IT assets is paramount.
The Infosec team at LTF secures
business applications through Security
by Design, and Zero Trust model with
ethical hacking, and 24/7 monitoring.

Governance and
Compliance
The Information Technology Strategy
Committee (ITSC), comprising
Board members, oversees LTF's
security posture through quarterly
meetings. Your Company holds
an ISO 27001:2022 certification,
ensuring that it’s Information
Security Management System (ISMS)
meets international standards. Your
Company’s digital platform features
a 3-tier Security Architecture with
in-built disaster recovery to protect
against ransomware, malware, and
data leakage. Meetings discussing IT
security infrastructure, applications,
governance, data security, and business
resiliency are held on a monthly basis.

Liquidity Risk
Heightened uncertainty fuelled by
shifting global trade dynamics and
evolving geopolitical conflicts has
increased volatility in funding markets.
Consequently, the need to proactively
manage liquidity risk - defined as the risk
of possessing insufficient liquid assets
to meet financial obligations - has never
been more critical.
Throughout FY26, LTF demonstrated
robust liquidity risk management,
maintaining positive cumulative gaps
consistently within the structural liquidity
statement for all buckets up to one
year. Your Company remained in full
compliance with both regulatory and
Board-defined limits for its Structural
Liquidity and Interest Rate Sensitivity
Statements, Liquidity Coverage Ratio
(LCR), and Stock Ratios. Furthermore,
our Contingency Funding Plan
remained highly effective; by proactively
monitoring Early Warning Indicators
(EWIs) on a daily basis, ensuring sufficient
liquidity to navigate period-specific
geopolitical events like Operation Sindoor
and ongoing Middle East conflicts.

Future-Proofing:
ESG, Climate, and
Geopolitical Risks
LTF is a pioneer in integrating emerging
risks into its core financial strategy.
Your Company recognises that climate
change can manifest as operational
interruptions or altered asset values.
Climate Integration
LTF performs detailed analyses
of variables like rainfall deviation
and reservoir storage capacity to
manage agricultural risk. Climate risk
considerations are now embedded
in it’s decision-making protocols and
periodic portfolio assessments.
ESG and ICAAP
In compliance with RBI directives, LTF
has established an Internal Capital
Adequacy Assessment Process (ICAAP)
that formally incorporates ESG risks.
Geopolitical Vigilance
LTF proactively monitors global,
domestic and economic uncertainties,
calibrating strategies to protect
collections at risk.

inter-alia major new circulars, KYC/AML compliance, and Fair Practice Codes.
Testing results are reported directly to the Audit Committee, ensuring a topdown culture of integrity.

Sustainability continues to be a core
strategic priority for LTF, embedded
across its business model, risk
management practices, and operational
processes. As a diversified retail
financier, your Company recognises
its role in enabling inclusive growth
while managing environmental and
social risks associated with its lending
and operations. During the year,
your Company strengthened ESG
integration into lending, portfolio
management, and governance
frameworks encompassing enhanced
assessment of environmental and
social risks, expansion of sustainable
finance initiatives, and continued focus
on responsible lending practices. Your
Company is also strengthening its
data and digital capabilities, including
automation of financed emissions
measurement, to enable more
accurate, timely, and forward-looking
risk assessment and disclosures. Your
Company’s sustainability approach
is supported by robust governance
structures, including Board-level
oversight and enterprise-wide
integration through the Sustainability
Steering Council and cross-functional
working groups, ensuring continued
alignment with evolving regulatory
requirements and global frameworks,
strengthening transparency and
disclosures. As your Company enters
its next phase of growth, sustainability
will continue to serve as a key
enabler of long-term value creation,
with a sharper focus on climate risk,
sustainable finance, and operational
efficiency driving resilient growth while
creating measurable environmental and
social impact.

Standalone
The Liquidity Coverage ratio improved by 91% increasing from 179% (for the quarter ended March 31, 2025) to 342% (for the
quarter ended March 31, 2026). This is primarily attributable to comparatively increase in the stock of High‑Quality Liquid Assets
(HQLA) and a concurrent decline in short‑term cash outflows, resulting in lower net cash outflows over the next 30‑days period.

Consolidated
RONW (or RoE) increased from 10.87% in FY25 to 11.25% in FY26 on the back of book growth of 25% and disbursement
growth of 38% YoY

From the CS & CSO’s Desk
"In line with the Risk-First, Tech-First
philosophy, your Company adopted
automation across critical environment data
collection, financed emissions and is among
the first in the NBFC sector to adopt a double
materiality assessment."

Apurva Rathod
Company Secretary & Chief Sustainability Officer

At LTF, sustainability is not just
about disclosures, it is embedded in
who we are and how we operate.
Our approach has evolved from a
disclosure-led framework to one
where sustainability is integrated
into risk management, business
strategy, and decision-making. The
objective being building an ESG
conscious Company.
As we move towards Lakshya 2031,
sustainability will become more
technology-led, risk-informed,
and impact-driven. I am proud
that your Company is ahead of
the curve in the NBFC sector,
which is reflected in our strong
sustainability performance and
various recognitions. In FY26, your
Company became a signatory to
the United Nations Global Compact
and hosted India’s first PCAF
Summit for the financial sector.
Your Company has automated
ESG data collection and financed
emissions tracking, and is among
the first in the NBFC sector to adopt
a double materiality assessment.

Statutory Reports

Financial Statements

Our Sustainability Journey

LTF is guided by its commitment to responsible growth, embedding E, S and G consideration into its business and
operations, supported by strong governance, robust risk management and a focus on delivering consistent, measurable and
long-term value.

Vision

At LTF, sustainability is backed by action, evidence and industry-first commitments. From being amongst
first to conduct Double Materiality Assessments to measuring business impact in monetary terms,
managing climate risks, achieving voluntary assurance ahead of regulatory mandates, and partnering
for carbon accounting your Company’s sustainability journey is defined by rigour, transparency and a
determination to lead from the front.

To be an environmentally and socially responsible financial institution built
on the foundation of ‘Assurance’, focused on generating sustainable longterm value for all stakeholders.
Phase III

FY23–FY26

Goals

Building an ESG-Conscious
Organisation

Ensure profitability with ethical, environmental and social responsibility
Phase II

Integrate Sustainability practices into LTF’s business, operations and value chain

FY21–FY22

Achieve ‘Best-in-Class’ ESG Ratings
Phase I
To deliver on this vision, your Company’s sustainability approach is anchored in three key focus areas.
Responsible Finance
Access to responsible and affordable credit is a cornerstone of inclusive growth. Your Company extends
credit across rural and urban geographies, serving diverse customer segments with products designed to
support livelihoods, enable climate-aligned financing, and empower women entrepreneurs.

People, Customer &
Communities
People are the promise, and
customers are the purpose.
Your Company is building an
organisation that is inclusive
from the inside out advancing
diversity, equity, and belonging
within the workforce, protecting
every customer’s rights,
and investing deeply in the
communities where it operates.

Statutory Reports

Enhanced
transparency, improved
regulatory readiness,
and stronger
sustainability controls

Your Company has a structured and forward-looking approach for embedding sustainability into its core business strategy.
The approach begins with a maturity and materiality assessment, through which key action areas and material ESG priorities
are identified, supported by leadership inputs. These insights shape your Company’s ESG purpose, vision, sustainability goals,
and cross functional defined KPIs, forming the foundation of its sustainability journey. Building on this, your Company's
sustainability roadmap is aligned with globally recognised standards and frameworks such as those of regulators and
international bodies, while also benchmarking against leading industry peers. This ensures that your Company’s approach
remains both compliant and competitive. The roadmap is then fed into an ESG risk-embedded strategy, where sustainability
is integrated into decision-making and risk management. This enables tangible outcomes including improved sustainable
business performance, robust risk assessment mechanisms, strengthened market positioning, enhanced quality of
disclosures.

Sustainable Finance
Framework

The ESG Policy provides the overarching
guidance for integrating environmental,
social and governance considerations
into lending, investments and
operational decision-making.

Your Company has a sustainable
finance framework that guides
financing decisions across portfolios,
built-in monitoring and reporting
mechanisms to track performance and
impact.

A key component is the ‘Exclusion List’,
which restricts financing of activities
with significant environmental, social or
ethical impacts.

Social Management
System (ESMS)
As part of your Company's commitment
to responsible business, your Company
has developed a comprehensive ESMS
ensuring the highest standards of
environmental integrity and social
equity across all lending and micro
finance activities. This policy ensures
that your business is conducted with
the highest standards of environmental
integrity and social equity across lending
activities and is applicable to TwoWheeler and micro finance portfolios.

The framework is aligned with the UN
Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
and supports the transition to a lowcarbon and inclusive economy.

Branch Due Diligence
— Sustainability at
the Design Stage
As your Company continues to expand
its branch network, sustainability
considerations are embedded into
the expansion process from the very
outset. Every new branch undergoes
a comprehensive branch due diligence
evaluation.
This structured checklist process ensures
that expansion decisions are aligned with
your Company’s sustainability priorities.
This ensures that every new branch
is not just commercially viable, but
environmentally and socially responsible
from day one.

Empowering Women Entrepreneurs

offering tailored home loans and loans against property
(LAP) for business growth. The initiative provides relaxed
eligibility criteria, such as better pricing and extended tenors
(up to 25 years for home loans and 12 years for industrial
LAP), targeting women who own or manage businesses

Active women borrowers
supported
These efforts are strengthening financial inclusion and enabling broader
participation in economic growth.

Powering Sustainability through Technology
At LTF, technology is the force that brings its sustainability ambitions to life. As sustainability becomes more data-intensive
and interconnected, digital capabilities enable your Company to move from intent to measurable impact. By embedding
advanced analytics, automation, and intelligent systems into its operations and decision-making, your Company is not
only improving efficiency but also enhancing transparency, expanding access, and strengthening risk management. This
integrated, tech-led approach allows your Company to scale responsibly while building a more resilient and future-ready
financial ecosystem.

Data-driven Environmental Stewardship
Your Company leverages advanced analytics, IoT, and cloud-based ESG platforms to track energy and water consumption
across its offices in real time. This granular visibility allows your Company to optimise usage, reduce carbon intensity, and
improve operational efficiency. At the portfolio level, your Company is using geospatial tools and satellite imagery to assess
risks, thus enabling business to support resilient and sustainable financing solutions.

Financial Statements

IMPACT STUDY

Business Impact Study

The True Value Bridge

In FY26, your Company conducted its 2nd Business Impact Study with the support
of an external consultant to assess the broader value created through its business
activities. The study marks an important milestone in your Company’s journey to
measure performance beyond financial outcomes and understand how its business
contributes to economic growth, social progress and environmental stewardship.
The assessment applies a True Value methodology, which evaluates how lending,
operations and digital innovation create measurable outcomes for customers, communities,
employees, the environment and the wider economy. By monetising these outcomes, the
study provides a holistic view of performance and helps quantify your Company’s True
Earnings – the value created for society alongside financial returns.

A key output of the study is the True Value Bridge, which combines your Company’s reported financial performance with the
monetised economic, social and environmental impacts generated through its activities.

How the Study Was Conducted
The assessment followed a structured and globally aligned methodology designed to ensure transparency, credibility
and comparability of results.

Stakeholder Engagement Framework:
Driving Insight-Led Strategy
Your Company’s stakeholder engagement framework functions as a dynamic
feedback loop, aligning external expectations with strategic priorities.
Stakeholders are viewed not merely as recipients of information, but as active
partners and co-creators of long-term value.
This collaborative approach has shaped Lakshya 2026 and continues to evolve
your Company’s next strategic roadmap, Lakshya 2031.
As part of its ongoing strategic efforts, your Company adopts a structured
approach to engagement, leveraging assessments and mechanisms to focus on
material risks and opportunities.

Meaningful

At LTF, stakeholder engagement is a
key pillar of long-term value creation
and responsible growth. Your Company
recognises that sustainable success
is closely linked to the strength,
transparency, and depth of its
relationships with customers, regulators,
investors, partners, communities, and
employees. Accordingly, stakeholder
engagement acts as a strategic lever,
shaping decision-making, strengthening
risk management, and enhancing
organisational resilience.

Statutory Reports

robust engagement architecture.

and real-time feedback. As a result,
stakeholder insights are systematically
embedded into business priorities,
enabling proactive risk identification,
sharper customer understanding, and
informed decisions. At the same time,

CSR Stakeholder
Assessment Study
To strengthen the effectiveness,
inclusivity, and long-term sustainability
of its CSR interventions, your
Company undertook a comprehensive

150

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

third-party stakeholder engagement
study. This initiative recognises that
meaningful development outcomes
require active participation of all
stakeholders across the ecosystem.
Accordingly, LTF engaged with an
independent and reputed external
agency to conduct an in-depth
assessment across its areas of
operation. The study covered a
diverse range of stakeholders,
including community members,

and grounded in on-the-ground
realities.
This initiative reflects a clear shift
from a top-down approach to
a participatory and co-creation
model. Stakeholders are no longer
seen merely as beneficiaries but as
active partners in the development
journey. By institutionalising thirdparty stakeholder engagement, your
Company has also established a
strong benchmark within the BFSI

its commitment to transparency,
accountability, and measurable social
impact.

Materiality Analysis

Double Materiality Assessment framework (DMA)

PRIORITISING
WHAT MATTERS
Your Company’s approach to

LTF’s Double Materiality framework evaluates sustainability through two complementary perspectives:

Your Company remains cognisant of evolving external
environment, including geo-political and macroeconomic
developments, which are continuously evaluated and
embedded into its risk and strategy frameworks. As part
of this ongoing process, the next comprehensive DMA is
planned for the upcoming financial year, ensuring that
material priorities remain relevant, forward-looking, and
complete.
Beyond prioritisation, the framework also enhances
transparency and accountability by improving risk visibility.
At the same time, a strong data governance architecture
ensures that information remains standardised, reliable,
and governed at source. This creates a solid foundation for
scalable growth aligned with Lakshya 2031. Together, these
elements move materiality beyond a compliance exercise,
positioning it as a strategic tool that drives decisions,
strengthens performance, and reinforces LTF’s commitment
to sustained value creation.

decision-enabling tool that strengthens responsiveness and
ensures that growth remains measured, responsible, and
aligned with evolving stakeholder expectations.

and disciplined execution across identified material areas.

F I N A N C I A L C A P I TA L

DRIVING
PERFORMANCE
WITH DISCIPLINE

At LTF, performance is shaped by a disciplined approach to growth that carefully balances scale, stability, and
efficiency. In a dynamic operating environment, your Company continues to strengthen profitability, enhance
portfolio quality and reinforce balance sheet resilience through prudent financial management, diversified
funding and robust risk governance. This is complemented by a sharp focus on improving operating efficiency,
optimising cost of funds and driving productivity through technology-led interventions.
At the same time, your Company remains committed to maintaining asset quality and ensuring that growth
is both, calibrated and sustainable. Strong underwriting practices, continuous portfolio monitoring and
conservative provisioning provide resilience across cycles, while a granular and diversified retail franchise
supports stable and predictable earnings. This integrated approach enables sustained growth, improves returns,
and supports responsible expansion, while remaining firmly aligned with long-term strategic priorities and
stakeholder expectations.

WHY
this matters

Statutory Reports

Financial Statements

WHAT
LTF focuses on
LTF continues to focus on strengthening performance, enhancing portfolio quality, and enabling scalable,
technology-led growth under Lakshya 2026. In this context, key areas of emphasis include:
Financial Performance and
Profitability Momentum

Scaling Retail and Strengthening
Portfolio Quality

Technology-led Efficiency and
Digital Enablement

Risk Governance and Asset
Quality Discipline

Sustainable and Responsible
Financing

Funding Strength and
Balance Sheet Resilience

Economic Value Creation and
Stakeholder Returns

Together, these focus areas provide a comprehensive view of how your
Company sustains performance, reinforces stability, and enables long-term
value creation.

HOW
LTF does it
LTF’s ability to deliver consistent performance is anchored in disciplined financial management, robust risk governance, and
prudent funding strategies. It maintains strong capital adequacy and conservative leverage levels, ensuring resilience and the
capacity to support continued growth. Rigorous underwriting and portfolio monitoring practices enable your Company to
preserve asset quality and proactively manage risks.
LTF also maintains a diversified funding base, supported by strong domestic and international credit ratings and access to
multiple funding sources, enabling efficient and cost-effective resource mobilisation. Its disciplined asset-liability management
framework ensures adequate liquidity and supports balance sheet stability across changing interest rate environments.
At the same time, your Company integrates sustainability considerations into its financing approach, ensuring that growth
remains responsible and aligned with long-term priorities. This balanced and disciplined approach enables LTF to strengthen
its institutional resilience, enhance operational efficiency, and deliver sustained value to stakeholders.

A strong and resilient financial foundation is essential to sustaining growth, maintaining stakeholder confidence, and
delivering consistent value across economic cycles. At LTF, financial capital management operates through a disciplined
cycle of mobilising capital from diversified funding sources, deploying it into productive lending opportunities, and recycling
it through repayments and collections to support continued credit expansion. This efficient capital rotation enables your
Company to scale its retail lending franchises while maintaining balance sheet stability, liquidity, and prudent risk discipline.
By maintaining strong capital buffers and disciplined financial management, LTF ensures that funds raised are deployed
responsibly and continuously redeployed to support new lending opportunities. This approach not only strengthens
operational resilience and supports investment in digital capabilities but also advances your Company’s broader purpose of
expanding access to credit, enabling entrepreneurship, and supporting inclusive economic development, while delivering
sustainable, long-term value for stakeholders.

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

L&T Finance

175

Corporate Overview

“Financial strength is not defined merely by

diversified and cost-effective
funding. We leveraged our focus
on PSL-compliant assets to unlock
lower-cost funding, driving a
substantial reduction in our overall
weighted average cost of funds.

by rigorous AI-led underwriting.

balance between secured and

FY26 marked a year of exceptional execution and disciplined growth for LTF, reflecting the strength of its diversified retail
franchise and sustained focus on building a scalable and technology-led lending platform. Despite operating in a dynamic
macroeconomic environment, your Company delivered strong, consistent growth across its lending portfolio while improving
profitability, strengthening margins, and enhancing capital efficiency.
This performance underscores the resilience of LTF’s business model and its ability to scale sustainably while maintaining
prudent risk discipline and operational efficiency.

Robust growth momentum and balance sheet expansion
During FY26, there was a structural
momentum to drive all-time highs
in retail disbursements and core
profitability through execution and
portfolio scaling. This resulted in strong
upward disbursements momentum and
strong expansion on ground.

The retail loan book represents the

sheet and is a key driver of interest
income and long-term profitability.

sustainable balance sheet expansion.

Governance Structure
At the apex of the governance framework, the Board of
Directors provides ultimate oversight over your Company’s
financial capital, serving as the highest governing authority
to provide strategic direction and management supervision.
It is directly responsible for setting the long-term strategic
direction, defining the enterprise-wide risk appetite, and
enforcing capital discipline.

The Risk Management Committee (RMC) ensures that
financial growth is matched with risk discipline. The
RMC formulates and periodically reviews the enterprisewide risk management policy, actively identifying and
mitigating credit, market, and liquidity risks. The Asset
Liability Management Committee (ALCO) functions
as the primary strategic decision-making body for
the asset-liability management of the enterprise from
a risk-return perspective. The ALCO provides critical
guidance on interest rates, liquidity management,
funding sources, and the investment of surplus funds.
While these formal structures govern the capital
framework, organizational cost optimization and
capital efficiency are further driven through cost forum
and other dynamic management forums through which
continuous strategic reviews are undertaken to ensure
sustained operational excellence.

Building structural strength through prudent financial management

Scaling Retail and Strengthening Portfolio Quality

Your Company continued to strengthen its financial position through disciplined capital allocation, prudent provisioning, and
active portfolio monitoring. Provision coverage remained robust at 67%, reinforcing balance sheet resilience and ensuring
adequate buffers against potential uncertainties.

LTF’s continued focus on building a high-quality, granular, and diversified retail portfolio has been central to its
transformation into a retail-focused lending franchise. In FY26, your Company successfully strengthened its retail franchise
while maintaining superior asset quality and portfolio resilience.

At the same time, your Company maintained a calibrated growth approach, balancing expansion with risk discipline to
ensure the long-term sustainability of returns.

managing risk, incorporating both established industry standards and internal best practices.

Integrated Annual Report 2025-26

At LTF, Sustainable and Responsible Financing remained a core strategic priority in FY26, aligned with your Company’s Lakshya
2026 roadmap and its broader vision of building a technology-led, risk-calibrated retail finance franchise. As India advances
toward its sustainable development ambitions alongside global frameworks championed by the G20 and the United Nations
Sustainable Development Goals, your Company continued to embed ESG principles into its lending, funding, and operational
ecosystems.
This integrated approach ensures that sustainability is not merely a compliance requirement but a strategic enabler of long-term
portfolio resilience, responsible growth, and stakeholder value creation.

Strengthening the Sustainable Finance Framework
and Governance
During FY26, your Company continued to operationalise its Sustainable
Finance Framework (SFF), enabling structured deployment of capital toward
environmentally and socially beneficial sectors. The framework aligns with
globally recognised principles issued by the ICMA and LMA and remains
validated through an independent Second-Party Opinion from CRISIL Ltd.
Your Company has a specialised working group that operates at the
functional level to track the performance of our sustainability-linked portfolio
and verify that allocated funds strictly align with both internal targets and
external ESG commitments. Governed by the SFF, the group systematically
monitors how environmental and social criteria are integrated into our
lending decisions. Through this continuous tracking, the working group
ensures accountability and transparency in the deployment of the capital,
actively measuring the impact of strategic investments. In FY26, ₹18,220 Cr
was directed towards sustainability-focused and priority sector lending.

The framework is anchored
on six core pillars:

M A N U FA C T U R E D A N D I N T E L L E C T U A L C A P I TA L

TRANSFORMING
LENDING THROUGH
DIGITAL INTELLIGENCE

LTF has built a phygital platform-led lending ecosystem that combines AI, advanced analytics, and the PLANET
App with a strong nationwide distribution network to deliver at scale and enable inclusive growth. At the core
is an integrated digital lending architecture, where proprietary AI engines, Project Cyclops, Project Helios, Project
Nostradamus, KAI (Kustomer AI), and Project Orion, power the entire lending lifecycle, from sourcing and
underwriting to portfolio monitoring, collections, and customer engagement. Together, these engines enable
faster decision-making, sharper risk management, and enhanced operational efficiency.
Customer and partner engagement is anchored through the PLANET App and Partner PLANET platforms,
enabling seamless, end-to-end digital journeys, real-time servicing, and enhanced transparency, significantly
accelerating digital adoption across segments.
Together, these capabilities position your Company to scale sustainably, enhance asset quality, improve
customer experience, and advance financial inclusion, while delivering measurable impact across key material
areas including innovation, compliance, and operational efficiency.

WHY
This Matters

WHAT
LTF Focuses on

Artificial Intelligence (AI) at LTF is no
longer merely a tool but a central pillar
of its transformation under the Lakshya
strategy, which seeks to reposition your
company as a Risk-first, Technologyfirst, Multi-product Retail Financier of
Choice. Its growth agenda is anchored
around diversifying customers and
products, underpinned by laser sharp
insights leveraging decision intelligence,
and executed through a scalable,
resilient digital architecture that enables
these insights to be effectively deployed
at customer level.

Your Company has built an integrated ecosystem of technology platforms,
AI-driven analytics and nationwide distribution infrastructure that powers a
digital lending model, combining digital intelligence with on-ground reach.

Against the backdrop of improved
digital penetration in the remote
corners of India, the credit options and
customer expectations continue to
evolve. In sync with digital revolution,
the regulatory landscape is maturing
and setting up a market for fair
competition across lenders. In response
to the evolving landscape, it is pertinent
for financial institutions to make their
decision-systems more effective and
responsible, augmenting the traditional
customer reach-out avenues. AI-driven
insights enable faster underwriting,
stronger portfolio monitoring, and more
personalised customer engagement.
While, digital platforms improve
operational efficiency and accessibility,
there is a need to make these systems
responsible and robust to comply with
regulatory frameworks while keeping
security and customer satisfaction at the
forefront for its growth strategy.
By investing in AI-powered systems,
as a modern technology infrastructure
and a strong distribution backbone,
your Company strengthens its ability
to scale responsibly, enhance customer
experience, and expand financial
inclusion across India.

Project Cyclops

Next-Gen Omni-Product, Omni-Customer Underwriting Engine

Designed for zero-downtime, even during peak seasonal disbursement cycles

Project Cyclops is LTF’s proprietary, in-house-developed AI and machine learning-based credit underwriting
engine. Conceptualised as a next-generation, omni-product and omni-customer underwriting platform, it
fundamentally reshapes credit risk assessment – transitioning from manual, fragmented evaluations to a
unified, automated and highly accurate decision-making framework.

Financial Statements

Project Helios

Project Nostradamus

Modernising the Core. Powering the Future.

AI-Driven, Real-Time Portfolio Intelligence Engine

Transforming Credit Decisioning through AI and Unified Underwriting Intelligence

Project Nostradamus is LTF’s proprietary, first-in-industry AI-powered automated portfolio management
engine. Designed as the intelligence layer that activates immediately after disbursement, it ensures that risk
oversight does not end at underwriting but continues dynamically across the entire lifecycle of every loan.

Project Helios is an AI-driven underwriting intelligence platform designed to modernise how credit decisions
are made across LTF’s lending businesses. As credit evaluation processes became increasingly data-intensive,
credit managers had to manually analyse multiple reports such as bureau reports, banking statements,
financial statements, deviation notes, and personal discussions.
Project Helios addresses this challenge by bringing these fragmented signals together into a single
intelligent decision support layer.

What Helios Enables
Unified Underwriting Intelligence

AI-Assisted Credit Decisioning

Improved Risk Visibility

Project Helios consolidates insights
from bureau data, banking
statements, financial documents,
deviations, probability of default (PD)
discussions, and other underwriting
signals into a single structured view,
reducing the need to manually
analyse multiple reports.

Through analysers such as Bureau
Analyser, Banking Analyser, Financial
Statement Analyser, and AI-assisted
Credit Assisted Memorandum (CAM),
Project Helios automatically highlights
key observations, risk indicators, and
probing questions to assist credit
managers during underwriting.

Project Helios leverages deviation
intelligence, PD insights and
cross-signal triangulation to
detect hidden risks and highlight
inconsistencies that may otherwise
be missed during manual analysis.

Automation of Manual
Underwriting Effort

Explainable and Governed
AI Layer

Processes such as EMI mapping,
financial document analysis, and
PD transcription, among others, are
automated, significantly reducing
manual effort and review time.

All insights generated by Project
Helios are explainable and auditable,
ensuring that credit managers remain
in control of final decisions while
benefiting from AI-driven assistance.

Strategic Impact
By integrating multiple underwriting signals into one
intelligent platform, Project Helios improves decision
consistency, credit TAT reduced by 33% in Self Employed
Non-Professionals (SENP) category and 33% in Self
Employed Professionals (SEP) category, and strengthens
credit governance. The platform enhances productivity of
credit teams while maintaining robust risk controls.
In essence, Project Helios acts as a unified underwriting
AI assistant, enabling faster, smarter, and more consistent
credit decisioning while preserving human oversight in the
underwriting process.

Project Orion

KAI

AI Conversational Co-Pilot for Portfolio Intelligence

KAI (Kustomer AI) is an Agentic AI-powered conversational assistant designed to transform how
customers engage with financial services through natural, human-like interactions. Built on Large
Language Model (LLM) technology, KAI enables customers to interact using free-flowing text or voice,
allowing them to query financial products, explore loan options, generate leads, and perform servicing
activities seamlessly.

Project Orion is LTF’s next-generation AI conversational assistant and the interface layer to Project
Nostradamus. While Project Nostradamus generates portfolio-wide predictive insights, Project Orion ensures
that these signals are instantly accessible and actionable for credit and business teams across FY26.

Democratising Enterprise Data
Project Orion removes reliance on technical teams
and static dashboards by enabling direct, self-serve
interaction with portfolio intelligence.
Plain-English Querying: Users can ask business
questions conversationally and receive precise,
instant responses

Dynamic Drill-Down: Enables exploration beyond
pre-built dashboards into segment, product, or
geography-level trends

Live Interpretation Layer: Translates
portfolio alerts into contextual summaries for
faster action

Core Capability Pillars
Conversational Intelligence: Understands
intent and context, enabling seamless human-like
interaction with enterprise data

Real-Time Awareness: Tracks live portfolio
performance data for FY26 and surfaces actionable
insights instantly

Workforce Empowerment: Accelerates reporting
and reduces turnaround times by enabling
independent analysis

Continuous Learning: Improves accuracy and
contextual relevance as usage expands

All AI interactions are unified under the KAI ecosystem. Currently KAI is active on the website chatbot
and voice outreach. We are evolving into a seamless omnichannel presence with KAI expanding into
integrations for WhatsApp and other digital engagement platforms.
From a technology perspective, KAI is powered by an agentic AI architecture, where LLMs orchestrate service execution
through tools exposed via a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. This allows the AI to efficiently invoke enterprise APIs
for operations such as eligibility checks, EMI calculations, lead generation, and loan servicing actions.
KAI also supports multilingual voice and text interactions in English, Hindi, Hinglish, Bengali, Tamil, Marathi, Telugu,
Kannada, Malayalam, Oriya, Gujarati, and Punjabi, enabling inclusive access for a wide range of users. LTF has a KAI Chat
Bot available on the website and KAI voice AI calling customers for sales and collections. With features such as LLM response
streaming, voice streaming, and conversational UI components, the platform delivers a fast and natural user experience that
closely resembles human conversation. We will be soon launching KAI on WhatsApp for customer acquisition, engagement,
and servicing.

Democratising Customer Experience
KAI represents a shift from traditional menu-driven digital interfaces to an open conversational experience, enabling
customers to interact with financial services using simple natural language.
Instead of navigating through multiple screens, forms, or IVR menus, customers can simply ask questions such as:

Core Capability Pillars
KAI is built around four foundational capability pillars that
enable it to deliver intelligent and efficient conversational
experiences.
Conversational Intelligence
At the core of KAI is an advanced conversational AI engine
powered by LLM technology. The system understands
natural language queries, interprets user intent, and
maintains conversational context to enable smooth
multi-step interactions.
Through contextual understanding and dynamic dialogue
management, KAI enables users to interact naturally rather
than following predefined scripts or rigid menu flows.
Real-Time Service Execution
Beyond answering questions, KAI is capable of executing
real-time financial services by orchestrating enterprise APIs
through its tool-based architecture. This allows the chatbot
to perform actions such as:
EMI calculations
Loan eligibility assessments
Lead creation
Loan detail retrieval
Servicing document downloads
This AI + API orchestration model transforms KAI from an
informational chatbot into a transactional digital assistant.

GeoGrid
To supplement traditional credit assessment, LTF has
developed a patent-pending Lifestyle Index and a
GeoGrid spatial model. The Lifestyle Index integrates
structured financial data with unstructured sources like
images and videos to produce a behavioural borrower
score, while GeoGrid provides hyper-local lending insights
by analysing urban development, building density, and
growth patterns.

PLANET 3.0

Digital Stakeholder Platforms

Scale and Adoption
The PLANET platform has achieved strong adoption and digital penetration:

PLANET App
Powering Digital Customer
Engagement
PLANET App is LTF’s flagship customer-facing digital platform,
designed to deliver a seamless, self-service financial experience.
Launched in March 2022, the app anchors your Company’s digital
transformation by shifting customer interactions from branch-led
servicing to a convenient, digital-first ecosystem.
Since its launch, the platform has evolved significantly,
progressing from a basic loan-servicing interface to PLANET
3.0 – a comprehensive digital engagement platform that
integrates loan origination, servicing, payments, and customer
support within a unified ecosystem. The enhanced platform offers
faster, more intuitive, and highly personalised journeys powered
by advanced analytics and conversational AI.

Partner PLANET App
Digitally Empowering the Dealer
Ecosystem
The Partner PLANET App, launched in October 2025, extends LTF’s
digital ecosystem to its dealer and distribution partners. While the
PLANET App serves retail customers, the Partner PLANET platform is
designed to simplify and digitise interactions with B2B stakeholders,
creating a more transparent and efficient dealer ecosystem.
The application acts as a one-stop digital interface for dealer
partners, enabling them to manage financing transactions, track
disbursements and access portfolio insights in real time. By digitising
dealer workflows, the platform improves operational visibility
while significantly reducing turnaround times across key financing
processes.

Key Capabilities
The Partner PLANET App provides dealers with integrated tools to manage their financing activities:
